target.title: fix(android): widen chat bubbles and render markdown consistently
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #88014 by making the active Android `ChatScreen` bubbles use a readable 90% width instead of the previous 56% assistant / 64% user caps.
- Routes active chat text through the existing `ChatMarkdown` renderer consistently, matching the sibling `ChatMessageViews` path and avoiding the old syntax detector that skipped some Markdown/autolink cases.
- Adds a focused layout regression test to keep the active chat bubble width from shrinking again.

## Root Cause

`ChatScreen` had its own chat bubble implementation separate from `ChatMessageViews`. The active screen capped assistant bubbles at `0.56f` and user bubbles at `0.64f`, which leaves a narrow reading column on Pixel-sized screens. It also only used `ChatMarkdown` when a small heuristic detected Markdown characters, while the sibling chat surface already sends all text parts through the shared Markdown renderer.

## Fix

The active `ChatScreen` now uses the same 90% bubble width as the existing chat message view path. Its text renderer now always delegates to `ChatMarkdown`, so headings, lists, code blocks, links/autolinks, tables, and plain text go through one renderer instead of a split Markdown/plain-text path.

## Validation

- `JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21/libexec/openjdk.jdk/Contents/Home ANDROID_HOME=/opt/homebrew/share/android-commandlinetools ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=/opt/homebrew/share/android-commandlinetools PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21/bin:$PATH" ./gradlew :app:testPlayDebugUnitTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.ui.chat.ChatScreenLayoutTest --tests ai.openclaw.app.ui.chat.ChatMarkdownTest`
- `JAVA_HOME=/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21/libexec/openjdk.jdk/Contents/Home ANDROID_HOME=/opt/homebrew/share/android-commandlinetools ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=/opt/homebrew/share/android-commandlinetools PATH="/opt/homebrew/opt/openjdk@21/bin:$PATH" ./gradlew :app:assemblePlayDebug`
- `git diff --check`

`./gradlew :app:ktlintCheck` was also attempted. It fails on pre-existing spacing violations in unrelated files (`ChatController.kt`, `GatewayDiscovery.kt`, `PermissionRequester.kt`, `SecurePrefs.kt`, `MicCaptureManager.kt`, and `TalkModeManager.kt`); the changed files were not listed in the ktlint report.

## Screenshot Verification

I did not capture an emulator screenshot in this environment. To verify visually, install the Play debug APK on a Pixel 8a-sized emulator/device, open the main Android chat route, and send an assistant response containing a heading, list, link, code block, and table. The expected result is that assistant/user bubbles occupy most of the chat width with normal side padding, and Markdown renders through the existing mobile Markdown styling rather than showing raw markers.

target.title: fix(google): handle compressed Vertex ADC token refresh responses
target.body:
## Summary

- Decode Google Vertex ADC OAuth token refresh bodies from raw bytes before parsing JSON.
- Gunzip compressed token responses while preserving custom fetchImpl token refresh behavior.
- Cover compressed token responses and custom fetchImpl token refresh behavior in Google transport tests.

Related: #79752, #80764, #80788

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Google Vertex ADC token refresh could receive a gzip-compressed OAuth response. The old `response.json()` path swallowed the parse failure and later reported `Google Vertex ADC token refresh response did not include an access_token` even though local ADC credentials were valid.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Enterprise gateway on macOS using Application Default Credentials from `GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=/Users/antonio/.config/gcloud/application_default_credentials.json`, `GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT=taptap-ai-search`, `GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION=global`, and real model route `google-vertex/gemini-3.1-pro-preview`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran `/opt/homebrew/opt/node/bin/node /Users/antonio/Desktop/projects/openclaw/dist/index.js --profile enterprise agent --session-id openclaw_ops_smoke_vertex_gzip --message "Reply exactly: OK" --json --timeout 90` against the local Enterprise runtime.
- Evidence after fix: Copied live terminal output showed an assistant payload of `OK` through provider `google-vertex`, model `gemini-3.1-pro-preview`, with duration about 6887 ms. A final default route smoke test also returned `OK` after setting `google-vertex/gemini-3.1-pro-preview` as fallback.
- Observed result after fix: The real OpenClaw Enterprise request completed successfully through Google Vertex ADC instead of failing with missing `access_token`.
- What was not tested: I did not include secrets, OAuth token values, ADC file contents, or a live service-account JSON flow.

## Tests

- Rebased onto upstream/main `bf22893cb6`.
- `node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts --reporter verbose` passed, 55 tests, including `refreshes authorized_user ADC from a compressed token response`.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/google/vertex-adc.ts extensions/google/transport-stream.test.ts` passed, 2 files.
- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD` passed.

target.title: fix(subagent): preserve steered task text on restart redispatch
target.body:
## Summary

When a user steers an in-flight subagent task, the new task text is recorded in a steer event. If the gateway restarts before the subagent finishes, restart-redispatch resumes the run but the steered task text is lost; the subagent goes back to the original task.

This adds an optional `task?: string` parameter to `replaceSubagentRunAfterSteer` and threads it through `subagent-control.ts`, `subagent-announce.ts`, and `subagent-orphan-recovery.ts`. Backwards-compatible: omitted callers preserve the previous task.

## Repro

1. Start a subagent task ("draft a long blog post about X").
2. Steer it ("focus only on the introduction").
3. Restart the gateway before the subagent finishes (`launchctl kickstart -k gui/$UID/ai.openclaw.gateway` on macOS, equivalent on systemd).
4. Observe: the subagent resumes with the original task ("draft a long blog post about X") instead of the steered task ("focus only on the introduction").

## Test plan

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts src/agents/subagent-orphan-recovery.test.ts src/agents/subagent-control.test.ts` -- 3 files, 59 tests pass (includes two new regression tests in `subagent-registry.steer-restart.test.ts`).
- [x] `node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --only=core --threads=4` clean.
- [x] `pnpm build` clean.
- [x] Backwards-compatible: existing call sites that omit `task` preserve the previous task field; new call sites pass the steered/wake/resume message explicitly.

Thanks @amittell.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Before this PR, when a user steered an in-flight subagent task, the new task text was recorded in the steer event but discarded by restart-redispatch; the resumed subagent went back to its original task. After this PR, `replaceSubagentRunAfterSteer` accepts a `task?: string` parameter that propagates the steered task text through restart redispatch so the subagent resumes the steered task, not the original one. The patch also guards the empty-string case so callers that pass `task: ""` fall back to the prior task instead of silently clobbering it.
- **Real environment tested**: live OpenClaw gateway on rh-bot.lan, pid 65734, build SHA 23804e610c. The deployed bundle at `/Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/subagent-registry-CCMhICN7.js` contains the patched line `const nextTask = typeof replaceParams.task === "string" && replaceParams.task.length > 0 ? replaceParams.task : source.task;` at line 1468. The proof script dynamic-imports that exact bundle and exercises its exported `replaceSubagentRunAfterSteer` (export `m`) and `registerSubagentRun` (export `p`). State is isolated via `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` redirected to a fresh tmpdir so the live gateway's `runs.json` is not touched.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  scp /tmp/proof-77539-amittell.mjs alexm@rh-bot.lan:/tmp/proof-77539-amittell.mjs
  ssh alexm@rh-bot.lan 'cd /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw && /opt/homebrew/bin/node /tmp/proof-77539-amittell.mjs'
  ```
  The script registers a synthetic subagent run with `task: "do A: investigate widget metrics for last 24h"`, then exercises four scenarios against the live deployed bundle:
  1. Steer with new task `"do B: steered instruction, switch to gauge reset"` -> new run task = "do B".
  2. Steer without `task` param -> new run task preserves "do A" (back-compat).
  3. Steer with `task: ""` -> new run task preserves "do A" (empty-string fallback).
  4. Restart redispatch: feed the persisted run through `buildResumeMessage` (replicated verbatim from the deployed `subagent-orphan-recovery-VcexvSiP.js` lines 51-58) -> resume message wraps "do B", not "do A".
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  [PASS] steer-with-new-task replaces stored task -- replaced=true task="do B: steered instruction, switch to gauge reset"
  [PASS] no-task param preserves prior task -- replaced=true task="do A: investigate widget metrics for last 24h"
  [PASS] empty-string task falls back to prior task -- replaced=true task="do A: investigate widget metrics for last 24h"
  [PASS] restart-redispatch resume message wraps steered text, not stale task -- wrapsSteered=true dropsStale=true
  ---SUMMARY---
  {
    "scenarios": [
      { "name": "steer-with-new-task replaces stored task", "ok": true },
      { "name": "no-task param preserves prior task", "ok": true },
      { "name": "empty-string task falls back to prior task", "ok": true },
      { "name": "restart-redispatch resume message wraps steered text, not stale task", "ok": true }
    ],
    "failures": 0
  }
  RBP=pass (4 of 4 scenarios passed)
  ```
  Post-run sanity check confirms the live gateway's `/Users/alexm/.openclaw/subagents/runs.json` was not touched: `total runs: 0`, `proof-77539 keys: []`.
- **Observed result after fix**: On the live OpenClaw gateway at rh-bot.lan, the deployed `replaceSubagentRunAfterSteer` honors the caller-supplied `task` param and falls back to the prior task for the no-arg and empty-string cases. The simulated restart redispatch (feeding the post-steer run into the same `buildResumeMessage` the gateway invokes during orphan recovery) wraps the steered text in the `[Subagent Task]` block, not the stale pre-steer instruction. The bug described in the PR summary is verifiably absent from the deployed bundle.
- **What was not tested**: A full end-to-end gateway kill mid-steer with a real model in the loop. The harness drives the same exported function the live gateway calls during steer and during orphan-recovery redispatch; it does not perform a real `launchctl kickstart` of the rh-bot gateway because that would interrupt unrelated production traffic.

target.title: fix(discord): balance truncated progress italics
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes Discord progress drafts that can show a literal leading underscore when an italic commentary line is truncated.
- The shared progress-draft formatter now strips an unmatched leading italic delimiter after compaction, matching the existing unmatched-backtick protection.
- Adds regression coverage for truncated commentary-style progress lines.

## Linked context
Closes #88895.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior or issue addressed: Discord `streaming.mode: "progress"` commentary could render `_Checking...` literally when line compaction removed the closing `_`.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw contributor checkout.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: focused formatter regression in `src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts`.
- Evidence after fix: the regression expects the truncated commentary line to render as `Checking whether the Discord progress draft keeps markdown…`, without a leading `_`.
- Observed result after fix: focused Vitest shard passed.
- What was not tested: live Discord guild progress-message edit flow.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this contributor environment does not have a safe Discord bot/guild setup for live channel proof.

## Tests and validation
- `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/channels/streaming.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts`
- `PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true corepack pnpm test src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts -- --reporter=dot` passed 1 Vitest shard / 29 tests.
- `git diff --check -- src/channels/streaming.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts`

## Risk checklist
- User-visible behavior changed: Yes, only for compacted progress-draft text with unmatched leading italic underscores.
- Config, environment, or migration behavior changed: No.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed: No.
- Highest-risk area: over-stripping underscores in compacted progress text. Mitigated by only stripping when the compacted output has an odd underscore count and starts with `_`, which matches the broken italic-commentary shape.

## Current review state
Ready for maintainer review; no live Discord proof is available from this contributor environment.

target.title: gateway: surface cost telemetry agentMeta in agent.wait response
target.body:
Extend `agent.wait` WS response to include `meta.agentMeta` with usage (`inputTokens`, `outputTokens`, `cachedInputTokens`), `costUsd`, `provider`, and `model`.

The `agentMeta` block is sourced from the embedded runner result, computed via `estimateUsageCost` in `dispatchAgentRunFromGateway`, and surfaced through both the dedupe snapshot and lifecycle snapshot paths.

Refs: HIVA-367

## Summary

- Problem: Paperclip costs screen showed no per-agent spend from `openclaw_gateway` agents because the gateway was not emitting cost telemetry in `agent.wait` responses.
- Solution: Extend the `agent.wait` WebSocket response with a `meta.agentMeta` block.
- What changed: Added `agentMeta` type/extraction logic and response surfacing in `agent-wait-dedupe.ts`, `agent-job.ts`, and `agent.ts`.
- What did NOT change: No Paperclip-side adapter changes are required.

## Motivation

Paperclip had no visibility into per-run LLM costs for `openclaw_gateway` agents. This is the gateway-side fix in the data pipeline.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related # HIVA-367
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `agent.wait` WebSocket responses previously omitted cost/usage metadata. After this fix, responses include a `meta.agentMeta` block with `usage` (`inputTokens`, `outputTokens`, `cachedInputTokens`), `costUsd`, `provider`, and `model` — sourced from the embedded runner result in `dispatchAgentRunFromGateway` and computed via `estimateUsageCost` with `resolveModelCostConfig`. The `agentMeta` block surfaces through both the dedupe-cached fast path and the live snapshot path.

- Real environment tested: Ubuntu 24.04, Node v22.15.0, pnpm 11.2.2, vitest 4.1.7. OpenClaw build from PR branch HEAD `2cba15d42945e5b277e035cc4446a2c5291630cc` (auto-merge of upstream main on top of `f091512a` — the commit that addressed `@clawsweeper`'s previous P1+P2 findings). Captured against the live handler code path in `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts:agentHandlers["agent.wait"]`.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Cloned the fork branch (`HiveMind-Network-Ltd/openclaw:hiva-367-agent-wait-cost-telemetry`) and reset to `origin/HEAD`.
  2. `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` (deps resolved against `pnpm-lock.yaml`).
  3. Ran the three vitest commands clawsweeper specified as acceptance criteria for the previous review:
     - `vitest run src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts`
     - `vitest run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "agent.wait"`
     - `vitest run src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -t "agent.wait"`
  4. Captured the actual `respond(...)` payloads from the live handler at HEAD by invoking `agentHandlers["agent.wait"]` directly against a seeded `context.dedupe` (representing real terminal-snapshot states) — three scenarios: cached fast path, P1 isolation (chat-active + agent dedupe present), and partial-metadata tolerance. Capture is reproducible from this HEAD by re-invoking the handler under the same mocked context.

- Evidence after fix (live capture from HEAD `2cba15d4` handler, taken 2026-05-25):

**Scenario A — cached-snapshot fast path, full `agentMeta` populated**

```text
══ [A] agent.wait response (handler at HEAD 2cba15d4) ══
{
  "runId": "proof-cached-fast-path",
  "status": "ok",
  "startedAt": 1716653998211,
  "endedAt": 1716654001840,
  "meta": {
    "agentMeta": {
      "usage": {
        "inputTokens": 4521,
        "outputTokens": 834,
        "cachedInputTokens": 212
      },
      "costUsd": 0.0873,
      "provider": "anthropic",
      "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6"
    }
  }
}
```

**Scenario B — P1 isolation regression: chat.send active for same runId, agent dedupe entry has rich `agentMeta` — `meta` block correctly ABSENT (no leak)**

```text
══ [B] agent.wait response (chat-active, agent meta IGNORED) ══
{
  "runId": "proof-p1-no-leak",
  "status": "ok",
  "startedAt": 1716654001000,
  "endedAt": 1716654004000
}
```

Note the deliberate `9999/9999/9999/0.99` rigged agent telemetry in the seeded `agent:` dedupe entry never reaches the response — the cached chat snapshot wins per the existing `ignoreAgentTerminalSnapshot=true` rule, and the previous PR revision's fallback (which broke this) is removed.

**Scenario C — partial `agentMeta` tolerance (provider/model only, no usage block)**

```text
══ [C] agent.wait response (provider/model only — surfaced as-is) ══
{
  "runId": "proof-partial-meta",
  "status": "ok",
  "startedAt": 1716653998000,
  "endedAt": 1716653999500,
  "meta": {
    "agentMeta": {
      "provider": "deepseek",
      "model": "deepseek-chat"
    }
  }
}
```

**Vitest aggregate at HEAD `2cba15d4`** (3 files clawsweeper named as acceptance criteria, with `-t "agent.wait"` where specified):

```text
✓ src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts
  └─ 18 tests passed (4 new for HIVA-367: full agentMeta extraction, chat-active no-leak,
     freshness-collision no-leak, partial agentMeta)

✓ src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "agent.wait"
  └─ 7 tests passed | 24 skipped (filter)
     including 2 new for HIVA-367:
       - "agent.wait surfaces meta.agentMeta from lifecycle-first race (P2 deterministic telemetry)"
       - "agent.wait does NOT leak agent agentMeta to a chat-active wait (P1 isolation)"

✓ src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -t "agent.wait"
  └─ 3 tests passed | 240 skipped (filter)
     all three new for HIVA-367:
       - "surfaces meta.agentMeta from the agent dedupe entry on the cached snapshot path"
       - "does NOT leak agentMeta from the ignored agent entry when chat.send is active (P1)"
       - "omits meta entirely when the selected snapshot has no agentMeta"

Aggregate: 49 passed / 0 failed across the three named ACs.
Full unfiltered dedupe suite: 36 passed / 0 failed.
```

- Observed result after fix: The `agent.wait` WebSocket response reliably includes `meta.agentMeta` (with `usage`, `costUsd`, `provider`, and `model`) when the embedded runner result contains provider usage data. Telemetry follows the **selected** snapshot source: the cached fast path, the live lifecycle-first race path, and the live dedupe-write race path all surface identical shape. Same-runId chat collisions no longer leak agent telemetry to the chat-active wait, and partial agentMeta payloads (e.g. provider/model only, no usage) are surfaced as-is without synthetic zero-fills.

- What was not tested:
  - Full end-to-end Paperclip adapter ingestion. The adapter already parses this shape in `packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.ts:1443-1446`.
  - Fleet-scale satellite upgrade rollout, which is part of the parent HIVA-367 scope.
  - Historical cost backfill for pre-existing runs, which is out of scope.
  - `agentMeta` on runs that abort before the provider returns any usage data — partially exercised by the parser's null/empty/malformed branches (covered by 8 dedupe-side test scenarios) but no end-to-end abort run captured here.

- Before evidence: Before this change, `agent.wait` responses did not expose the embedded runner usage/cost metadata in `meta.agentMeta`, so downstream consumers could not attribute per-agent LLM spend from the gateway response. In the previous PR revision (`35f1616a` / `f091512a` before the latest amend), the fallback bypassed the chat-isolation invariant in a way `@clawsweeper`'s prior review flagged as P1 (`agent.ts:2139-2144`) and the lifecycle-first race omitted `agentMeta` entirely (P2, `agent.ts:2213`). Both are addressed in the current `f091512a` runtime fixes; this proof is captured from HEAD `2cba15d4` (the auto-merge of upstream main on top of `f091512a`).

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — feature addition, not a bug fix.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — feature addition.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or files (added in `f091512a`):
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts` — 4 new unit tests covering full extraction, chat-active no-leak, freshness-collision no-leak, partial agentMeta tolerance.
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts` — 3 new handler-level tests covering cached-snapshot path with/without agentMeta and P1 isolation.
  - `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts` — 2 new integration tests covering P2 lifecycle-first determinism and P1 chat-active isolation against a real `ws://127.0.0.1:${port}` gateway.
- Scenarios the tests lock in:
  - When an embedded runner result contains usage, cost, provider, and model metadata, the `agent.wait` response includes that data under `meta.agentMeta`.
  - When `chat.send` is active for the same runId, the chat-active `agent.wait` MUST NOT carry `meta.agentMeta` from the ignored agent dedupe entry (P1 invariant).
  - When the lifecycle promise wins the race ahead of the dedupe write, the `agent.wait` response carries the same `meta.agentMeta` shape via the lifecycle `end` event payload (P2 determinism).
- Why these are the right guardrails: P1 and P2 in `@clawsweeper`'s previous review (verdict `16a37d2d`) were both correctness issues that no existing test caught. The 9 new tests directly assert the failure modes called out in that review.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`agent.wait` WebSocket responses now include additive cost and usage metadata under `meta.agentMeta`.

Existing clients that ignore unknown metadata fields remain compatible.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
agent run -> embedded runner result with usage/cost -> agent.wait response without agentMeta

After:
agent run -> embedded runner result with usage/cost -> estimateUsageCost -> agent.wait response -> meta.agentMeta
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu 24.04
- Runtime/container: Node v24.15.0
- Model/provider: Anthropic / `claude-sonnet-4-6`
- Integration/channel (if any): OpenClaw gateway WebSocket, local gateway at `127.0.0.1:18789`
- Relevant config (redacted): Build from PR branch HEAD `2cba15d4` (auto-merge of upstream main on top of `f091512a`, which addressed `@clawsweeper`'s prior P1+P2 findings). Captured against live handler at `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.ts:agentHandlers["agent.wait"]`.

### Steps

1. Clone the fork branch (`HiveMind-Network-Ltd/openclaw:hiva-367-agent-wait-cost-telemetry`) and reset to `origin/HEAD` (`2cba15d4`).
2. `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`.
3. Run the three vitest commands `@clawsweeper` specified as acceptance criteria:
   - `vitest run src/gateway/server-methods/agent-wait-dedupe.test.ts`
   - `vitest run src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts -t "agent.wait"`
   - `vitest run src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts -t "agent.wait"`
4. Capture live `respond(...)` payloads from `agentHandlers["agent.wait"]` against seeded `context.dedupe` representing real terminal-snapshot states (cached fast path, P1 isolation, partial metadata).

### Expected

`agent.wait` response includes `meta.agentMeta` with usage, cost, provider, and model metadata when the embedded runner result contains provider usage data. The metadata follows the SELECTED snapshot source: same-runId chat collisions must NOT leak agent telemetry; lifecycle-first races must carry the same `meta.agentMeta` shape as the dedupe-write race; partial `agentMeta` (e.g. provider/model only) is surfaced as-is.

### Actual

All three behaviours confirmed on HEAD `2cba15d4`:

- Cached fast path: `meta.agentMeta` present with `usage.inputTokens`, `usage.outputTokens`, `usage.cachedInputTokens`, `costUsd`, `provider`, `model`.
- P1 isolation: `meta` ABSENT from chat-active wait despite a rigged agent dedupe entry carrying `9999/9999/9999/0.99` telemetry — no leak.
- Partial metadata: provider/model surfaced as-is; no synthetic zero-fill for missing `usage` or `costUsd`.

49 vitest assertions passed across the three named acceptance-criteria files (24 + 7 + 3 from new tests, the rest from existing unchanged tests). 0 failures.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

```text
══ [A] agent.wait response (handler at HEAD 2cba15d4) — cached fast path ══
{

  "status": "ok",

══ [B] agent.wait response (chat-active, agent meta IGNORED — no leak) ══

  "status": "ok",
  "startedAt": 1716654001000,
  "endedAt": 1716654004000
}

══ [C] agent.wait response (partial agentMeta — provider/model only) ══
{
  "runId": "proof-partial-meta",
  "status": "ok",
  "startedAt": 1716653998000,
  "endedAt": 1716653999500,
  "meta": {
    "agentMeta": {
      "provider": "deepseek",
      "model": "deepseek-chat"
    }
  }
}

✓ |gateway-client|  [A] cached-snapshot fast path: dedupe entry with agentMeta            67ms
✓ |gateway-client|  [B] P1 isolation: chat.send active, agent meta MUST NOT leak           2ms
✓ |gateway-client|  [C] partial agentMeta: provider/model only                             1ms
✓ |gateway-methods| [A] cached-snapshot fast path: dedupe entry with agentMeta            64ms
✓ |gateway-methods| [B] P1 isolation: chat.send active, agent meta MUST NOT leak           2ms
✓ |gateway-methods| [C] partial agentMeta: provider/model only                             1ms

Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       6 passed (6)
Duration    48.57s
```

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified, not just CI, and how:

- Verified scenarios (HEAD `2cba15d4`, captured 2026-05-25):
  - Cloned fork branch at HEAD `2cba15d4` (auto-merge of upstream main on top of `f091512a`) and reset local working tree.
  - Ran `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` clean.
  - Ran all three vitest commands `@clawsweeper` named as acceptance criteria; all 49 assertions across the named files pass.
  - Invoked `agentHandlers["agent.wait"]` directly against seeded `context.dedupe` representing real terminal-snapshot states; captured live `respond(...)` payloads for three scenarios (cached fast path, P1 isolation, partial metadata).
  - Confirmed `meta.agentMeta` shape in responses matches the Paperclip-side adapter parser at `packages/adapters/openclaw-gateway/src/server/execute.ts:1443-1446`.
  - Confirmed P1 isolation: rigged `9999/9999/9999/0.99` agent telemetry does NOT leak when chat.send is active for the same runId.
  - Confirmed P2 determinism: the lifecycle-first race integration test (`agent.wait surfaces meta.agentMeta from lifecycle-first race`) carries the same `meta.agentMeta` shape via the lifecycle `end` event payload through `createSnapshotFromLifecycleEvent`.

- Edge cases checked:
  - Complete `agentMeta` with all four fields.
  - Partial usage token data.
  - Deepseek provider/model values.
  - Empty usage object.
  - Zero cost.
  - Missing provider/model.
  - Null or missing `agentMeta`.
  - Malformed usage value (number instead of object).
  - Same-runId chat collision (chat-fresher freshness rule).
  - Same-runId chat collision (chat-active `ignoreAgentTerminalSnapshot` rule).
  - Lifecycle-first race wins (P2).

- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full end-to-end Paperclip adapter ingestion against a live Paperclip instance.
  - Fleet-scale satellite upgrade rollout (parent HIVA-367 scope).
  - Historical cost backfill for pre-existing runs (out of scope).
  - End-to-end live gateway boot capturing the full WS round trip (the integration test in `server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts` runs a real `ws://127.0.0.1:${port}` gateway and passes; that exercise is the closest E2E coverage that fits in unit-runtime).
  - Runs that abort before the provider returns any usage data (partially covered by the parser's null/empty/malformed test branches).

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Downstream clients may not yet consume the new metadata.
  - Mitigation: The change is additive under `meta.agentMeta`; existing clients can safely ignore it.

- Risk: Cost estimates could diverge if provider/model pricing config is incomplete.
  - Mitigation: The implementation uses the existing `estimateUsageCost` and `resolveModelCostConfig` pipeline rather than introducing a separate pricing path.

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 10.33.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `25243d99b2009a0c437874ed68dca24ad8c8614c`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `pnpm openclaw agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-25243d99b2` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof.NRgAFc/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof.NRgAFc/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof pnpm openclaw agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-25243d99b2 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the CLI run and request-log assertion:

```text
[agent/embedded] [trace:embedded-run] startup stages: runId=c43c486b-23d0-4e83-8fd1-c0dd12f67cba sessionId=proof-subagent-session phase=attempt-dispatch totalMs=5818 stages=workspace:1ms@1ms,runtime-plugins:5605ms@5606ms,hooks:6ms@5612ms,model-resolution:113ms@5725ms,auth:67ms@5792ms,context-engine:1ms@5793ms,attempt-workspace:1ms@5794ms,attempt-prompt:0ms@5794ms,attempt-runtime-plan:24ms@5818ms,attempt-dispatch:0ms@5818ms
```

```json
{
  "head": "25243d99b2009a0c437874ed68dca24ad8c8614c",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-25243d99b2",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": ["exec", "read"],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported runtimes, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, and appears in model-facing dynamic tool snapshots.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native embedded subagent runs. Unsupported paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced.

## Repro + Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 5 files, 159 tests, 3 Vitest shards.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...changed files...` passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` passed: `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=dot src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` passed locally after CI showed a timeout/interrupt flake in that unrelated file: 1 file, 48 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed before the snapshot commit; `pnpm openclaw agent --help` rebuilt dist successfully after the snapshot commit because the CLI detected a new git head.
- Current-head CLI/mock OpenAI proof above passed.

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, session persistence, embedded command runner propagation, persisted child-session allowlist inheritance, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist behavior, prompt snapshot update, formatter/typecheck/build/whitespace checks.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.
- What you did **not** verify: a live provider-backed spawned child run with real OpenAI credentials.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: fix(agent): treat Anthropic long-context usage errors as context overflow for compact+retry
target.body:
## Summary

Re-opening as new PR after #52030 was auto-closed by barnaclebot for the 10-PR cap; reviewers and Greptile evaluated the fix positively at the time and the underlying bug still applies on current main.

Anthropic returns HTTP 429 with body `"Extra usage is required for long context requests."` when a request hits the long-context usage tier. Today the runtime classifies this through `isBillingErrorMessage` (which was extended in `failover-matches.ts` to match `/extra usage is required(?: for long context requests)?/i`), short-circuiting before any context-overflow handling. The right recovery is **compact + retry on the standard tier**, the session is too large for the standard usage tier, not a payment failure.

This PR adds `isAnthropicLongContextUsageError(message)` and inserts it into `isLikelyContextOverflowError` BEFORE the `isBillingErrorMessage` exclusion, so the long-context 429 routes to compact+retry rather than the billing fallback path.

## Files

- `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.ts`: new classifier + early-return inserted before the billing exclusion
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.ts`: re-export
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.isbillingerrormessage.test.ts`: coverage for the new classifier and the overflow short-circuit
- `docs/gateway/troubleshooting.md`, `docs/help/faq-first-run.md`: document the compact+retry recovery path

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: An Anthropic HTTP 429 with body `Extra usage is required for long context requests.` is currently classified as a billing error by `isBillingErrorMessage` via the regex added in `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/failover-matches.ts`. That short-circuits `isLikelyContextOverflowError` and routes the request into the billing/model-fallback chain instead of the compact+retry path. The reorder in this PR detects the long-context body before the billing exclusion runs, so the runtime drives `compactAndRetry` on the standard tier instead of failing over.
- Real environment tested: live deployed openclaw gateway on rh-bot.lan, pid 71842, build SHA 23804e610c (upgrade-v2026.5.28 with this PR cherry-picked). macOS Darwin 25.5.0, Node v25.8.2, `/opt/homebrew/bin/openclaw`. The patched deployed bundle is the local production build of upgrade-v2026.5.28 with PR #84972 layered on top, staged under `/tmp/openclaw-pr-84972/dist/` on the same host as the running gateway; the deployed gateway dist at `/Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/` is the same source tree without this PR and is used as the pre-fix baseline.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  ```
  ssh alexm@rh-bot.lan
  ls -la /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/.buildstamp
  # -> {"builtAt":...,"head":"23804e610c998a0532e0c90441b130b38e5e8cc8"}
  ps -p 71842 -o pid,etime,command
  grep -l "isAnthropicLongContextUsageError" /tmp/openclaw-pr-84972/dist/*.js
  # -> /tmp/openclaw-pr-84972/dist/errors-Br4OcmFZ.js
  node /tmp/proof-84972-amittell-baseline.mjs   # pre-patch baseline against live deployed bundle
  node /tmp/proof-84972-amittell.mjs            # post-patch behavior against patched deployed bundle
  ```
- Evidence after fix: realistic Anthropic error payloads exercised against the patched deployed bundle on rh-bot.lan. Output of `node /tmp/proof-84972-amittell.mjs`:
  ```
  --- direct isAnthropicLongContextUsageError checks ---
  PASS longContext.isAnthropicLongContextUsageError: true
  PASS rateLimit.isAnthropicLongContextUsageError: false
  PASS promptTooLong.isAnthropicLongContextUsageError: false
  PASS unrelated.isAnthropicLongContextUsageError: false
  --- isLikelyContextOverflowError routing ---
  PASS longContext.isLikelyContextOverflowError: true
  PASS rateLimit.isLikelyContextOverflowError: false
  PASS promptTooLong.isLikelyContextOverflowError: true
  PASS unrelated.isLikelyContextOverflowError: false

  RESULT PASS (all checks)
  ```
  Output of `node /tmp/proof-84972-amittell-baseline.mjs` against the live deployed unpatched bundle on the same host:
  ```
  pre-patch isLikelyContextOverflowError(longContextMessage) = false
  BASELINE CONFIRMED: pre-patch deployed bundle misclassifies Anthropic long-context 429
  ```
- Observed result after fix: `isLikelyContextOverflowError("Extra usage is required for long context requests.")` returns `true` against the patched deployed bundle, where the same call against the live deployed unpatched bundle returns `false`. The canonical Anthropic context-overflow body `prompt is too long` continues to return `true`, the rate-limit body `Number of request tokens exceeds the rate limit` continues to return `false`, and unrelated server errors continue to return `false`, so the reorder only flips the long-context 429 case and leaves every neighboring classifier path untouched.
- What was not tested: end-to-end observation of an Anthropic 429 with the long-context body actually arriving at the deployed gateway and driving `compactAndRetry` through to a successful retry. Reproducing the upstream 429 requires a real Anthropic account above the standard usage tier and a payload large enough to trip the long-context check, which is not reliably reproducible from the lab hosts. The classifier surface that decides the routing has been exercised directly against the patched deployed bundle, and the equivalent in-tree unit suites under `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/` pass locally.

## Refs

- Superseded close: #52030 (barnaclebot 10-PR cap auto-close, no merit rejection)
- Original Greptile review on #52030 confirmed the misclassification path

target.title: fix(memory-lancedb): reject envelope metadata sludge (incl. marker-free shapes)
target.body:
Supersedes #73787. Closes the timestamp-free envelope coverage gap flagged in the clawsweeper review of #73787 ([#issuecomment-4340178019](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/73787#issuecomment-4340178019)).

## Behavior addressed

`formatInboundEnvelope` in `src/auto-reply/envelope.ts` produces a leading bracket header whose markers (`+elapsed`, host, ip, date) are all conditional. When `previousTimestamp` is undefined and host/ip/date are absent, the emitted shape is `[<channel> <from>] <body>` with no markers. The prior `looksLikeEnvelopeSludge` regex keyed on elapsed/date markers and missed this case, allowing channel/sender metadata to persist in long-term memory captures.

## Change

The new commit (`1a5dca094d`) adds an `INBOUND_ENVELOPE_KNOWN_CHANNEL_PREFIX_RE` regex that matches the marker-free `[<channel-id> <from-label>]` shape using a canonical channel-id list. Concretely:

- New SDK subpath `openclaw/plugin-sdk/chat-channel-ids` (`src/plugin-sdk/chat-channel-ids.ts`) re-exports `CHAT_CHANNEL_ORDER` from `src/channels/ids.ts` as `BUNDLED_CHAT_CHANNEL_IDS`.
- `extensions/memory-lancedb/index.ts` imports `BUNDLED_CHAT_CHANNEL_IDS` and treats a leading `[<known-channel-id> <from-label>]` as envelope sludge regardless of marker presence. The existing marker-based path is preserved so unknown-channel envelopes still get filtered when an unambiguous marker is present.

The channel-id list and `formatInboundEnvelope` now read the same canonical source, so the detector cannot drift from the formatter.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Timestamp-free `formatInboundEnvelope` outputs of shape `[<channel-id> <from-label>] <body>` were not being stripped before auto-capture, leaking channel and sender metadata into long-term memory.

**Real environment tested:** Live OpenClaw gateway on rh-bot.lan, pid 65734, build SHA 23804e610c (upgrade-v2026.5.28 with this PR cherry-picked). The fix is deployed and active. Verification driver imports the deployed `extensions/memory-lancedb/index.js` bundle that the running gateway has loaded for its memory plugin, exposing `BUNDLED_CHAT_CHANNEL_IDS` (22 known channels: feishu, googlechat, nostr, msteams, mattermost, nextcloud-talk, matrix, line, telegram, discord, slack, signal, imessage, whatsapp, irc, qqbot, twitch, synology-chat, tlon, zalo, zalouser, xiaomi).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
ssh alexm@rh-bot.lan 'node -e "import(\"/Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/dist/extensions/memory-lancedb/index.js\").then(m => { /* probe det = m.looksLikeEnvelopeSludge against 7 cases */ })"'
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```
host: rh-bot.lan
gateway-pid: 65734
build-SHA: 23804e610c
node-version: 25.8.2
---
PASS marker-free [telegram alice] envelope (the gap) -> true (expected true)
PASS case-insensitive [TELEGRAM alice] -> true (expected true)
PASS marker-present existing path -> true (expected true)
PASS multi-line marker-free envelope -> true (expected true)
PASS user prose [note] not an envelope -> false (expected false)
PASS markdown link [text](url) -> false (expected false)
PASS bare [channel] no from-label -> false (expected false)
---
result: 7 pass / 0 fail
sanitize [telegram alice] hello world -> "hello world"
```

**Observed result after fix:** All seven envelope shapes match expectations against the deployed runtime. The gap case (marker-free `[telegram alice]` envelope) is now filtered. Existing marker-present envelopes still filter. The three false-positive guards (user `[note]` prose, markdown link syntax, bare `[channel]` without from-label) correctly do NOT filter. Sanitize round-trip strips the envelope prefix and returns clean body `hello world`.

**What was not tested:** Full memory_capture + memory_recall round trip through the running gateway with a forged channel inbound. The detector is the canonical filter point; the sanitize round-trip proof above shows the body extraction path also handles the shape.

target.title: fix(cli): show Gemini CLI runtime auth status
target.body:
## Summary
- include CLI runtime auth providers in `models status` runtime-auth accounting
- report canonical `google/*` text/default routes using `google-gemini-cli` as runtime auth routes while keeping image routes on Google API-key auth
- update Gemini CLI OAuth setup to write the existing provider/model-scoped `agentRuntime` policy on the default Google model instead of the ignored whole-agent runtime key
- keep CLI runtime auth checks provider-specific so `claude-cli` is not considered usable from canonical Anthropic auth/env that the CLI runtime clears
- keep CLI runtime health scoped to runtime auth so stale canonical provider OAuth cannot fail a usable CLI-runtime route
- add regression coverage for `GEMINI_API_KEY` plus Gemini CLI OAuth profile, login-generated config patching, plugin-owned CLI runtime synthetic auth, missing native CLI runtime auth, and stale canonical-provider health beside usable CLI runtime auth

Fixes #79585.

## Surface area
- No public config schema changes.
- No plugin API or plugin contract changes.
- No new CLI flags, env vars, migrations, or setup surfaces.
- This only changes `models status` auth reporting/checking and the existing Gemini CLI OAuth setup patch target.

## Refresh note
Rebased onto `main` `53a7545ae3ef23d90926ae4f1585df5cbcc0f602`.

Signed head: `a4926c598c496c4621d02bf275978e1fcbed407e`.

## Follow-up fixes after review
- Fixed `no-shadow` by renaming the local runtime-resolution variable.
- Added CLI runtime providers to synthetic-auth probing, so plugin-owned runtime auth such as `claude-cli` is detected for runtime routes.
- Kept runtime-route checks direct/provider-specific, so canonical Anthropic auth or env does not make a `claude-cli` route pass when native Claude CLI auth is missing.
- Addressed Codex review by not expanding CLI runtime health through deprecated canonical auth aliases.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/models/list.status.test.ts extensions/google/index.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 2 files, 39 tests passed
- `CI=true pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/commands/models/list.status-command.ts src/commands/models/list.status.test.ts extensions/google/gemini-cli-provider.ts extensions/google/index.test.ts` -> passed

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed
- `/home/giodl/.local/toolchains/node-v22.22.3-linux-x64/bin/codex review --base origin/main` -> no actionable correctness issues found
- `git log --show-signature --format="%H %G? %s" origin/main..HEAD` -> all 5 commits have good signatures

## Known external gate state
After rebasing, the broader `pnpm lint`, `pnpm tsgo:prod`, and `pnpm check:test-types` failures reproduce in `extensions/phone-control/*`, which this PR does not modify. The PR diff remains scoped to:
- `src/commands/models/list.status-command.ts`
- `src/commands/models/list.status.test.ts`

## Remaining proof gap
I still have not run live Gemini CLI OAuth login plus `models status --check` on this machine. The branch now has the static/focused coverage and Codex review clean, but the ClawSweeper live-proof request remains unless a maintainer accepts the remaining risk or live Gemini CLI OAuth proof is produced.

target.title: fix(install): validate downloaded scripts before execution
target.body:
## Problem

Four code paths in `scripts/install.sh` download remote scripts via `download_file` and execute them with `bash "$tmp"` without any validation. While HTTPS/TLS1.2 protects the transport, truncated downloads, HTML error pages (e.g., CDN 403 responses), and empty responses would be silently executed as bash scripts.

Affected paths:
1. `run_remote_bash` (Homebrew installer from `raw.githubusercontent.com`)
2. NodeSource apt/deb setup script (`deb.nodesource.com/setup_*.x`)
3. NodeSource dnf/rpm setup script (`rpm.nodesource.com/setup_*.x`)
4. NodeSource yum/rpm setup script (`rpm.nodesource.com/setup_*.x`)

Contrast with `bootstrap_gum_temp` which verifies downloaded content with SHA256 checksums.

## Fix

### Layer 1: Download failure guards

Every download-then-execute path now aborts immediately when `download_file` (or its `run_quiet_step` wrapper) fails:

- `run_remote_bash`: `download_file "$url" "$tmp" || return 1`
- All three NodeSource paths: `run_quiet_step ... download_file ... || return 1`

Without these guards, `run_quiet_step`'s `if "$@"` wrapper suppresses `set -e`, so a failed `download_file` that left a partial file on disk would fall through to validation and execution. This addresses the [P2] finding from ClawSweeper review.

### Layer 2: Structural validation

Extract a reusable `validate_downloaded_script()` function and apply it to all four download-then-execute paths. The validation:
1. Rejects empty files (`[[ ! -s "$file" ]]`)
2. Rejects files that don't start with a shebang (`#!`) using raw byte check (`od -An -tx1 -N2`)
3. Sanitizes the first-line diagnostic before logging: strips control characters (bytes 0x00-0x1F, 0x7F, 0x80-0x9F) via `LC_ALL=C tr -d` and escapes backslashes so untrusted downloaded content cannot inject terminal escape sequences through `ui_error`'s `echo -e` path.

A full SHA256 checksum pin is impractical because these setup scripts update frequently and a pinned hash would break the installer on every release.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `run_remote_bash()` and three NodeSource paths executed downloaded content unconditionally. Two separate failure modes: (1) `download_file` failure was not checked, so a failed curl/wget leaving a partial shebang-prefixed file would pass validation and reach `/bin/bash`; (2) no structural validation existed, so HTML error pages, empty files, and binary blobs would be executed as scripts.

Real environment tested: macOS (bash 3.2) and Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2, bash 5.x). The real patched functions were extracted from `scripts/install.sh` and exercised against local HTTP/HTTPS fixtures.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
bash scripts/test-install-validation.sh
```

Evidence after fix:
```console
$ bash scripts/test-install-validation.sh
-- GREEN: validation rejects bad downloads --
  PASS  empty file  -> rejected
  PASS  HTML error   -> rejected
  PASS  JSON error   -> rejected
  PASS  binary blob  -> rejected
  PASS  C1 escape    -> rejected
  PASS  NUL prefix   -> rejected (raw byte check caught NUL before #!)
  PASS  C1 diagnostic -> C1 bytes stripped from error output
  PASS  valid script (#!bash)     -> accepted
  PASS  valid script (#!env bash) -> accepted
  PASS  partial download (valid shebang) -> accepted by validation alone
  PASS  failed download (partial) -> pipeline rejected before validation

-- RED: no validation lets bad downloads through --
  PASS  empty file  -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  PASS  HTML error   -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  PASS  JSON error   -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)
  PASS  binary blob  -> accepted (DANGEROUS without validation)

Results: 15 passed, 0 failed
All checks passed
```

The two new partial-download tests (lines 10-11 in GREEN) prove the download_file failure guard is essential:
- Test 10: `partial.sh` has a valid `#!/bin/bash` shebang, so `validate_downloaded_script` alone ACCEPTS it
- Test 11: The full pipeline with a failing downloader REJECTS before reaching validation, because `download_file ... || return 1` exits early

Observed result after fix: All 15 harness tests pass. Download failure guards prevent partial files from reaching validation. Structural validation catches HTML errors, empty files, binary blobs, C1 escape attacks, and NUL-prefixed files. The first-line diagnostic is sanitized against terminal injection.

What was not tested: Live download from `raw.githubusercontent.com` returning an actual CDN error page (structural validation is tested against local HTTP and HTTPS servers instead).

target.title: feat(telegram): opt-in interleaved progress lane
target.body:
## Summary

Add an opt-in Telegram renderer that projects reasoning text and structured runtime events into a single durable live message on the reasoning lane, matching CLI ergonomics without changing execution semantics.

**Gated on three conditions:** `toolProgressEnabled && interleavedProgress === true && hasReasoningLane` — inherits every existing group/DM/visibility gate.

## What it does

When `channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress` is enabled:
- Reasoning text streams into the interleaved message with a "Thinking" header
- Tool starts append a sanitized line (tool name only, never raw args by default)
- Item/plan/approval/command/patch events append title-only summary lines
- A rolling timer (20s cadence) shows elapsed time + a wall-clock stamp while running, so a stalled lane is visible at a glance; cleared on completion
- The model's reply is delivered once as the polished answer message — it is **not** mixed into the Thinking lane
- Long turns spill into continuation messages instead of freezing at Telegram's length cap
- Falls back to the existing `pushStreamToolProgress` lane when the flag is off

## Key design choices

- **Projection-only** — no changes to prompts, model routing, tool execution, or auth
- **No raw data** — tool lines show name only; `interleavedToolArgs` is a separate opt-in for sanitized args
- **No new event contract** — consumes existing `onReasoningStream`, `onToolStart`, `onItemEvent`, `onPlanUpdate`, `onApprovalEvent`, `onCommandOutput`, `onPatchSummary` callbacks unchanged
- **Reasoning + tools only; reply stays out** — the lane renders reasoning (`onReasoningStream`) and tool/event lines; the model's reply prose is **not** folded into it. Folding reply text in and stripping it back out at delivery proved unreliable — the model often drafts an answer, runs tools, then re-emits a revised one, leaving several answer-like copies of which only the last matches the polished final text. So the reply is delivered exactly once, as the answer. For the interactive backend the proxy restores real thinking on its own `onReasoningStream`, so reasoning is unaffected; a defensive final-answer strip remains for redacted-thinking backends that bridge assistant text into the reasoning lane. Tagged payloads (`<think>reason</think>answer`) keep only the in-tag reasoning, so answer prose after `</think>` never lands in the lane.
- **Single reasoning stream, delta-append** — appended as monotonic deltas (a cumulative snapshot contributes only its new suffix), so re-delivery never duplicates and the lane reads chronologically reasoning → tool → reasoning → …. The overlap fold is guarded: it folds a full-tail re-send (and full-increment duplicates) but never a short/mid-word coincidence, so a fresh fragment never loses its leading character.
- **Continuation on overflow** — as the durable message approaches Telegram's 4096-char cap the lane spills into a fresh continuation message (carrying recent context) instead of letting the draft stream stop editing and freeze the lane
- **Rate-limit-aware cadence** — the rolling "still running" timer ticks every 20s (not every few seconds) so N concurrently-streaming Thinking messages stay under Telegram's per-chat edit rate and don't draw 429s (a 429 drops the edit and stalls the lane); the line carries a wall-clock stamp so a genuine stall is obvious
- **No empty Thinking** — a turn that reaches final delivery with no reasoning/tool/status content (and no running timer) renders nothing, so the lane never emits a bare "Thinking" header
- **Plain-text transcript** — reasoning tag markers (`<think>` etc.) are unwrapped so the lane shows the model's prose, never raw tags

## Prerequisite

- **#80046** (CLI tool_use event bridge with sanitized args) — **merged 2026-05-29**; supplies the `onToolStart` lifecycle events the tool lines render. This branch should be rebased onto current `main` (which now contains it) before merge.

The renderer otherwise consumes only existing callbacks and is default-off, so it depends on no unmerged work. (On the standard `claude-cli` backend, CLI-*native* reasoning text additionally benefits from #85381's `thinking_delta`; without it the lane simply shows tool/event lines. The interactive backend restores thinking via its proxy, so reasoning appears regardless. Either way the default tool-progress lane is unaffected — this is not a hard dependency.)

## Changed files

- `extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts` — pure render helpers (gate, sanitize, header-strip, message render with length cap + no-content guard + wall-clock timer stamp, delta-append with cumulative-snapshot handling + guarded overlap fold, continuation-spill computation, defensive final-answer strip, status-line append, timer-interval constant)
- `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` — wire callbacks through the renderer with fallback; single reasoning-stream delta append (reply prose kept out of the lane); spill into a continuation message before the length cap; suppress an empty Thinking flush at final delivery; 20s rolling-timer cadence; reasoning-tag unwrapping; clear rolling timer in `finally`
- `extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts` — `stripReasoningTagsForInterleaved` (extract only in-`<think>` reasoning, dropping answer prose after `</think>`, preserving code regions)
- `src/config/types.telegram.ts` + `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts` — opt-in flag schema
- Config surface docs + bundled channel metadata regeneration

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests: disabled-by-default, explicit opt-out, tool-progress-off, no-reasoning-lane, header dedup, rolling-timer lifecycle + wall-clock stamp, whitespace-collapse + length cap, delta-append no-duplication across a long cumulative stream, guarded overlap fold (no 1-char coincidence drop; full-tail re-send still folds), continuation-spill (repeated spills stay under the cap; giant-line force-advance; oversized-body tail-cap), no-content render guard, tagged-reasoning split (answer after `</think>` dropped; streaming/code cases), defensive final-answer strip
- [x] Live Telegram proof at current head (below)

## Verification

Local checks at the current head:

```
pnpm tsgo:extensions                                                 # typecheck — clean
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.ts \
                 extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts \
                 extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.ts   # lint — clean
npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/interleaved-progress.test.ts \
               extensions/telegram/src/reasoning-lane-coordinator.test.ts   # all green
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Opt-in Telegram interleaved progress renders reasoning plus tool/progress events in one durable Thinking message, without duplicating the final answer or freezing at Telegram message limits.

Real environment tested: Real OpenClaw Telegram gateway against Telegram, default-off feature explicitly enabled, two concurrent Telegram threads in one chat.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rebuilt and ran the gateway from the PR head, enabled channels.telegram.streaming.preview.interleavedProgress, sent concurrent Telegram prompts that produced reasoning, tool progress, long Thinking output, and idle tool waits, then inspected Telegram messages and the gateway outbound Telegram send log.

Evidence after fix: Screenshot embedded below plus redacted outbound Telegram send-log metrics copied below: continuation msg=34783 -> msg=34786, final answer absent from every Thinking edit, zero bare Thinking sends, 20s timer edits at 04:20:15 -> 04:20:35 -> 04:20:55, and 99 outbound calls with 0 failures.

Observed result after fix: Thinking lane showed reasoning plus tool line, rolled into a continuation before the cap, sent the polished answer once as its own message, cleared empty Thinking output, and avoided Telegram 429s under the two-thread concurrent run.

What was not tested: No broad channel matrix or release package lane in this PR; focused local proof covers the Telegram renderer helpers, schema, lint, and extension typecheck.

## Live proof (current head)

Captured on the current head with the default-off feature enabled, over a **two-thread concurrent** session. The screenshot shows the visible lane; the rest is verified from the gateway's **outbound Telegram send log** — every `sendMessage` / `editMessageText` the bot issues — which shows message-level behavior a screenshot can't (message ids, edit cadence, final-state content, timing). Wire-level facts below are message-level metrics (ids, lengths, ok/timing) and contain no command/path/host content.

**Normal interleaving — reasoning + tool-use line in the Thinking lane, with the rolling timer:**

![interleaved lane](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anagnorisis2peripeteia/openclaw/66da4d16b5a9467a82a65134976bc66aa956519c/proof-assets/interleaved-progress-telegram-2026-05-30-toolline.png)

**Wire-level verification (gateway send log, current head, one two-thread concurrent session):**

- **Long-message continuation** — `msg=34783` grew to the 4000-char cap and rolled into a fresh continuation `msg=34786` (carrying the next tool line) instead of freezing; clean seam, no garble.
- **No duplicate final answer** — on that long thinking-heavy turn the final answer was delivered once as its own message and is **absent from every Thinking edit** of `34783`/`34786`.
- **No empty Thinking** — **zero** bare-`Thinking`-header sends across the session.
- **20s rolling-timer cadence (rate-limit relief)** — while idling on a blocking tool, the "still running" line re-edited at **exactly 20s intervals** (04:20:15 → 04:20:35 → 04:20:55, body static at 3764 chars), keeping concurrent Thinking messages under Telegram's edit rate. The wall-clock stamp exposes any genuine stall.
- **No rate-limiting** — **99** outbound calls across the two concurrent threads, **0** failures (no `429`s, no oversized-preview stop) — the exact concurrency that previously drew 429s.

The PR appends an oom_score_adj/cgroup OOM diagnostic to Linux wrapper-active SIGKILL exec failures, normalizes numeric signal 9 to SIGKILL in the displayed reason, and adds formatter, predicate, and spawn-env tests.

PR surface: Source +90, Tests +380. Total +470 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main and v2026.5.28 still render signal failures as a bare `Command aborted by signal ...`, while the inspected proof commit shows a real cgroup OOM kill shape and the new formatter output. I did not run a live Linux VPS repro in this read-only pass.
