target.title: fix(codex): avoid guardian review for local models
target.body:
## Summary
- Downgrade Codex app-server Guardian/model-backed approval reviewers to user/OpenClaw approvals unless the effective reviewer context is clearly OpenAI.
- Treat explicit custom providers, provider-qualified local model refs, persisted binding model providers, side-question forks, and bound conversation turns as fail-closed for Guardian/model-backed review.
- Add OpenClaw internal exec auto-review for Codex app-server command approvals only for `tools.exec.mode=auto`, simple parsed command approvals, and an explicit `tools.exec.reviewer.model` under `openai/...`; unsafe control commands, amendments, security-audit suppression, unparsed commands, and reviewer ask decisions fall back to plugin/human approval.
- Refresh the Plugin SDK API baseline for the exec auto-review contract and keep current-main extension lint cleanup green.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: local/custom model Codex app-server auto approval no longer tries to use Codex Guardian/model-backed review, and internal exec auto-review requires an explicitly OpenAI-qualified reviewer model.
Real environment tested: delegated Blacksmith Testbox for focused Vitest plus changed gate; local static generated-baseline/hash and diff checks.

target.title: Tag embedded subagent gap-fill rows as delivery mirrors
target.body:
Fixes #87329

## User-facing bug
Subagent announce / embedded handoff gap-fill can append a user-visible assistant mirror using the child runtime provider and model metadata, such as `anthropic/claude-*`, instead of marking the row as an OpenClaw transcript-only delivery mirror. Replay filters only exclude `provider: "openclaw"` with `model: "delivery-mirror"` / `gateway-injected`, so a display-only mirror can later be replayed back into a provider turn. In conversations with provider-managed thinking/signature blocks, that stale mirror can surface as errors like "thinking blocks cannot be modified."

## Exact repro
Source-level reproduction:

1. Run `persistCliTurnTranscript` for an embedded assistant gap-fill with:
   - `embeddedAssistantGapFill: true`
   - `result.meta.agentMeta.provider: "claude-cli"` or another runtime provider
   - visible final text in `result.meta.finalAssistantVisibleText`
2. Before this fix, the appended transcript assistant row used `api: "cli"`, `provider: "claude-cli"`, and `model: "opus"`.
3. That row is user-visible display state, not provider conversation state, so replay filters do not identify it as transcript-only.
4. After this fix, the same gap-fill row is written as `api: "openai-responses"`, `provider: "openclaw"`, and `model: "delivery-mirror"`.

## Fix summary
- Changed embedded assistant gap-fill transcript persistence to write assistant mirrors as `openclaw/delivery-mirror`.
- Kept normal CLI transcript persistence unchanged: non-gap-fill CLI replies still preserve real CLI provider/model metadata and usage.
- Added regression coverage so the gap-fill path now asserts the transcript-only mirror metadata.

## Targeted tests
- Extended `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts` to prove embedded gap-fill rows are tagged as `openclaw/delivery-mirror`.
- Re-ran replay-history coverage to confirm `delivery-mirror` rows remain excluded from embedded provider replay.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Embedded subagent assistant gap-fill rows are now stored as OpenClaw delivery mirrors, so provider replay filters do not treat display-only text as provider conversation state.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/subagent-announce-delivery-metadata`, commit `3ba0619f`, rebased onto `origin/main` at `be127318`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/replay-history.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`
- `codex review --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix:
- Command execution Vitest passed: `Test Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 62 passed (62)`.
- Replay-history plus command execution Vitest passed: `Test Files 3 passed (3)`, `Tests 87 passed (87)`.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `codex review --base origin/main` passed with no actionable regressions found.

Observed result after fix: Embedded gap-fill transcript rows use `api: "openai-responses"`, `provider: "openclaw"`, and `model: "delivery-mirror"` while normal CLI transcript rows still preserve real provider/model metadata.

What was not tested: `pnpm check:changed` did not reach changed-file checks because dependency installation hit npm registry DNS failures (`ENOTFOUND`) in the sandbox.

## Not tested / known gaps
- No live provider-managed thinking/signature conversation was replayed end to end; coverage is through transcript persistence and replay-history tests.

target.title: Preserve Mattermost streamed blocks in block mode
target.body:
Fixes #87322

## User-facing bug

Mattermost used the live draft-preview path even when the account opted into completed-block delivery. That meant logical block replies were edited into one draft preview instead of being delivered as stable visible block posts. In `streaming.mode: "block"`, finalizing the preview could also collapse a longer accumulated draft to only the last final segment.

## Exact repro

1. Configure Mattermost with preview streaming enabled and `blockStreaming: true`, or set `streaming.mode: "block"` and produce a final payload that is only the last streamed segment.
2. Send a prompt that produces multiple visible text blocks around tool/progress events.
3. Observe Mattermost editing/suppressing the block surface instead of preserving visible block delivery or a validated accumulated preview.

## Fix summary

- Added a Mattermost streaming delivery policy that keeps draft preview and block delivery mutually exclusive unless Mattermost explicitly chooses block delivery.
- Explicit Mattermost `blockStreaming: true` now disables draft preview and passes `disableBlockStreaming: false` to the reply pipeline.
- Active draft previews remain active even when `agents.defaults.blockStreamingDefault` is on unless Mattermost explicitly opts into block delivery.
- Partial-reply `delta`/`replace`/`phase` metadata now reaches channel preview callbacks, so Mattermost can distinguish accumulated final-answer transcript updates from stale progress text.
- Mattermost block-preview finalization can reuse accumulated preview text only when the partial stream proves it is an accumulated final-answer transcript, and the accumulated candidate passes the same chunk/limit validation as normal finals.
- Tool/progress suppression is now tied to an active draft preview so block delivery does not hide default progress messages.

## Targeted tests

- Mattermost monitor tests cover streaming policy selection, accumulated preview finalization, and draft-preview delivery.
- Auto-reply tests cover partial-reply metadata propagation through CLI assistant events and dispatch.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Mattermost block-streaming configurations now preserve visible completed blocks instead of editing them into one draft preview or collapsing the final preview to only the last segment.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/mattermost-block-streaming-posts`, commit `7672dcdd`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.test.ts -t "resolveMattermostStreamingDeliveryPolicy|resolveMattermostPreviewFinalText|deliverMattermostReplyWithDraftPreview"`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.test.ts`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts -t "bridges CLI assistant agent events into onPartialReply|preserves embedded assistant partial metadata|bridges CLI assistant agent events into onReasoningStream"`
- `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs --run --maxWorkers 1 src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-cli-dispatch.test.ts`
- `codex review --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix:
- Targeted Mattermost and auto-reply Vitest commands passed.
- `git diff --check` passed.
- `codex review --base origin/main` found no blocking regression after the local fixes.

Observed result after fix: Mattermost only uses draft preview when the delivery policy selects preview mode, and explicit block streaming keeps completed blocks visible while progress suppression remains tied to active draft preview.

What was not tested: Live Mattermost server delivery was not run. `pnpm check:changed` is blocked by dependency install/network in this environment. `tsgo` typecheck is blocked because `node_modules/.bin/tsgo` is missing in this checkout.

## Not tested / known gaps

- Live Mattermost server delivery.
- `pnpm check:changed`, blocked by dependency install/network in this environment.
- `tsgo` typecheck, blocked because `node_modules/.bin/tsgo` is missing in this checkout.

## AI-assisted disclosure

This PR was prepared with AI assistance and local command/test verification.

target.title: fix(state): harden sqlite path caching
target.body:
## Summary

- resolve explicit SQLite database paths before caching state/agent handles
- set SQLite `busy_timeout` before WAL setup so cold concurrent opens wait instead of failing immediately
- add state and agent DB regressions for relative explicit paths across cwd changes

## Verification

Behavior addressed: explicit relative SQLite DB paths could alias one cached handle across different current working directories, and cold concurrent opens could fail with `database is locked` before write traffic started.

Real environment tested: local macOS gwt worktree plus Blacksmith Testbox-through-Crabbox Linux changed gate.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/state/openclaw-state-db.test.ts src/state/openclaw-agent-db.test.ts`
- local stress harness: `24 workers x 300 writes x 4 KiB blobs`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- corepack pnpm check:changed`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 24/24; stress harness completed 7,200 writes with 0 failures and `PRAGMA integrity_check` returned `ok` for both state and agent DBs; Testbox-through-Crabbox `tbx_01kszxmnh0cxezsss0c6bjfb2g` exited 0.

Observed result after fix: relative `state.sqlite` / `agent.sqlite` paths opened from different cwd values produce separate DB handles/files, and concurrent open/write pressure no longer reports lock failures.

What was not tested: full repo `pnpm verify`, live app runtime traffic, and non-macOS local filesystem behavior beyond the Linux Testbox changed gate.

target.title: fix(gemini): bridge OAuth profiles into CLI runtime
target.body:
## Summary

- opt `google-gemini-cli` into explicit OpenClaw auth-profile forwarding
- refresh/load the selected OAuth profile before CLI backend preparation
- materialize selected Gemini OAuth credentials into an isolated `GEMINI_CLI_HOME/.gemini/oauth_creds.json` for the `gemini` subprocess
- keep Gemini CLI auth epochs profile-owned when the bridge is active

Fixes #88742.

## Proof

HEAD: `d884722e235342d016211e82bc4085d228261259`

Validation log: `/tmp/openclaw-pr88742-validation.log`

Commands:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts --reporter=dot
pnpm -s exec oxfmt --check extensions/google/cli-backend.ts extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/plugin-sdk/cli-backend.ts src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts src/plugins/types.ts
pnpm -s exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/cli-backends.test.ts src/agents/cli-backends.ts src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/plugin-sdk/cli-backend.ts src/plugins/cli-backend.types.ts src/plugins/types.ts extensions/google/cli-backend.ts extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Relevant output:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  59 passed (59)
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  3 passed (3)
All matched files use the correct format.
```

Source-level behavior proof: `/tmp/openclaw-pr88742-proof.log`

- `extensions/google/setup-api.test.ts` proves selected OAuth credentials produce an isolated Gemini CLI home and writes `.gemini/oauth_creds.json` without logging token material.
- `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts` proves canonical `google/*` models routed through `google-gemini-cli` select the `google-gemini-cli:*` auth profile before `runCliAgent`.

Not tested:

- Full `pnpm -s exec tsgo --noEmit` is blocked on current HEAD by pre-existing errors outside this patch:

```text
src/config/io.ts(192,7): error TS2527: The inferred type of 'configWritePostCommitRollback' references an inaccessible 'unique symbol' type. A type annotation is necessary.
src/secrets/config-io.ts(8,17): error TS4058: Return type of exported function has or is using name 'configWritePostCommitRollback' from external module ".../src/config/io" but cannot be named.
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `google-gemini-cli` backend preparation receives a selected OpenClaw `google-gemini-cli:*` OAuth profile, materializes that OAuth profile into an isolated `GEMINI_CLI_HOME/.gemini/oauth_creds.json`, and passes that isolated home to the real `gemini` subprocess instead of relying on ambient `~/.gemini` state.
- **Real environment tested**: Local Linux workstation, PR branch `fix/88742-gemini-cli-oauth-bridge`, head `d884722e235342d016211e82bc4085d228261259`, OpenClaw `2026.5.31 (7150977)`, `@google/gemini-cli` binary available as `/usr/local/bin/gemini`, real Gemini CLI version `0.40.1`. Token values and account identifiers were not printed.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```text
cd /home/rev/projects/openclaw-prs/pr-88742-gemini-cli-oauth-bridge
pnpm install
pnpm build
confirmed backend registration path:
  rg -n 'buildGoogleGeminiCliBackend|prepareExecution|CliBackendPlugin|cliBackends' src packages extensions/google | head -120
  nl -ba extensions/google/cli-backend.ts | sed -n '1,155p'
created direct backend proof harness:
  .tmp-pr88748-gemini-backend-proof.mts
ran:
  BAD_HOME=/tmp/tmp.6jUzYBOeWy PROFILE='google-gemini-cli:jason.allen.oneal@gmail.com' pnpm exec tsx ./.tmp-pr88748-gemini-backend-proof.mts 2>&1 | tee /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.log
sanitized:
  sed -E -e 's/ya29\.[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/<redacted-access-token>/g' -e 's#1//[A-Za-z0-9._-]+#<redacted-refresh-token>#g' -e 's/eyJ[A-Za-z0-9._-]+/<redacted-jwt>/g' -e 's/AIza[[:alnum:]_-]+/<redacted-google-api-key>/g' /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.log > /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.redacted.log
verified no obvious token material remained:
  grep -E 'ya29\.|1//|eyJ|AIza' /tmp/pr88748-direct-backend-proof.redacted.log
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```text
=== PR 88748 direct Gemini CLI backend proof ===
backend_id=google-gemini-cli
profile=google-gemini-cli:jason.allen.oneal@gmail.com
bad_parent_GEMINI_CLI_HOME=/tmp/tmp.6jUzYBOeWy
prepared_GEMINI_CLI_HOME=/tmp/openclaw/gemini-cli-home-m4iizB
prepared_home_is_bad_parent=false
home_mode=700
gemini_dir_mode=700
oauth_creds_path=/tmp/openclaw/gemini-cli-home-m4iizB/.gemini/oauth_creds.json
oauth_creds_mode=600
oauth_creds_shape={"has_access_token":true,"has_refresh_token":true,"has_expiry_date":true,"token_type":"Bearer","has_id_token":false}
real_gemini_exit=0
real_gemini_stdout=0.40.1
real_gemini_stderr=
cleanup_removed_home=true
```

- **Observed result after fix**: `buildGoogleGeminiCliBackend().prepareExecution(...)` accepted a `google-gemini-cli` OAuth profile, created a private isolated Gemini CLI home, wrote `.gemini/oauth_creds.json` with Bearer OAuth credential shape and restrictive file permissions, returned a `GEMINI_CLI_HOME` different from the deliberately bad parent home, and a real `gemini` subprocess started successfully with that prepared environment. Cleanup removed the isolated home afterward.
- **What was not tested**: The proof intentionally did not print raw Google OAuth access tokens, refresh tokens, ID tokens, or private account data. The subprocess proof used `gemini --version` to prove the real Gemini CLI binary starts under the prepared `GEMINI_CLI_HOME`.

target.title: fix(cli): show Gemini CLI runtime auth status
target.body:
## Summary
- include CLI runtime auth providers in `models status` runtime-auth accounting
- report canonical `google/*` text/default routes using `google-gemini-cli` as runtime auth routes while keeping image routes on Google API-key auth
- update Gemini CLI OAuth setup to write the existing provider/model-scoped `agentRuntime` policy on the default Google model instead of the ignored whole-agent runtime key
- keep CLI runtime auth checks provider-specific so `claude-cli` is not considered usable from canonical Anthropic auth/env that the CLI runtime clears
- keep CLI runtime health scoped to runtime auth so stale canonical provider OAuth cannot fail a usable CLI-runtime route
- add regression coverage for `GEMINI_API_KEY` plus Gemini CLI OAuth profile, login-generated config patching, plugin-owned CLI runtime synthetic auth, missing native CLI runtime auth, and stale canonical-provider health beside usable CLI runtime auth

Fixes #79585.

## Surface area
- No public config schema changes.
- No plugin API or plugin contract changes.
- No new CLI flags, env vars, migrations, or setup surfaces.
- This only changes `models status` auth reporting/checking and the existing Gemini CLI OAuth setup patch target.

## Refresh note
Rebased onto `main` `53a7545ae3ef23d90926ae4f1585df5cbcc0f602`.

Signed head: `a4926c598c496c4621d02bf275978e1fcbed407e`.

## Follow-up fixes after review
- Fixed `no-shadow` by renaming the local runtime-resolution variable.
- Added CLI runtime providers to synthetic-auth probing, so plugin-owned runtime auth such as `claude-cli` is detected for runtime routes.
- Kept runtime-route checks direct/provider-specific, so canonical Anthropic auth or env does not make a `claude-cli` route pass when native Claude CLI auth is missing.
- Addressed Codex review by not expanding CLI runtime health through deprecated canonical auth aliases.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/models/list.status.test.ts extensions/google/index.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 2 files, 39 tests passed
- `CI=true pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/commands/models/list.status-command.ts src/commands/models/list.status.test.ts extensions/google/gemini-cli-provider.ts extensions/google/index.test.ts` -> passed
- `CI=true pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/commands/models/list.status-command.ts src/commands/models/list.status.test.ts extensions/google/gemini-cli-provider.ts extensions/google/index.test.ts` -> 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed
- `/home/giodl/.local/toolchains/node-v22.22.3-linux-x64/bin/codex review --base origin/main` -> no actionable correctness issues found
- `git log --show-signature --format="%H %G? %s" origin/main..HEAD` -> all 5 commits have good signatures

## Known external gate state
After rebasing, the broader `pnpm lint`, `pnpm tsgo:prod`, and `pnpm check:test-types` failures reproduce in `extensions/phone-control/*`, which this PR does not modify. The PR diff remains scoped to:
- `src/commands/models/list.status-command.ts`
- `src/commands/models/list.status.test.ts`
- `extensions/google/index.test.ts`

## Remaining proof gap
I still have not run live Gemini CLI OAuth login plus `models status --check` on this machine. The branch now has the static/focused coverage and Codex review clean, but the ClawSweeper live-proof request remains unless a maintainer accepts the remaining risk or live Gemini CLI OAuth proof is produced.

No merge performed.

target.title: feat(context-engine): pass runtime settings into lifecycle
target.body:
## Summary

AI-assisted change: yes. Forge/Codex produced the implementation and PR shepherding with OpenAI `gpt-5.5`; ragesaq reviewed the direction and required production-grade proof before merge.

Pass a typed `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` contract through the context-engine lifecycle for embedded, CLI, and Codex app-server runs. Context engines now receive a versioned snapshot of runtime mode, model/provider state, host capabilities, token limits, and diagnostics instead of only the assemble-time `model` string.

## Changes

- Add `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` and `ContextEngineRuntimeMode` in `src/context-engine/types.ts`.
- Add `buildContextEngineRuntimeSettings()` in `src/context-engine/runtime-settings.ts`.
- Thread `runtimeSettings` through bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, compact, and maintenance paths.
- Export the runtime-settings contract and errors through the public plugin SDK barrel.
- Preserve strict legacy context engines: if an engine rejects `runtimeSettings` as an unknown key, OpenClaw retries the lifecycle call without it instead of quarantining the engine.
- Document the public context-engine runtime-settings contract in `docs/concepts/context-engine.md` and `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`.
- Update the plugin SDK export contract test for the new public runtime-settings error exports.
- Add configured-slot proof that a resolved context engine receives runtimeSettings across bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintenance, and compact.
- Preserve the same overflow compaction runtimeSettings into the immediately-following post-compaction maintenance call.

## Current Head

Current PR head: `a767005b615a2e8eb6400d5c7ecd7e4cd2c942d8`.

Scope cleanup: the unrelated Copilot Gemini model-id commit `747f09e19610` has been removed from this PR by rewriting the branch over the current `release/2026.5.31` base. The current PR stack is 8 context-engine/runtime-settings commits, 20 files changed, and no `extensions/github-copilot` files in the diff.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Context engines configured through `plugins.slots.contextEngine` receive the typed `runtimeSettings` payload after the fix across bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintenance, and compact lifecycle hooks.
- **Real environment tested**: Final-head OpenClaw checkout at `a767005b615a2e8eb6400d5c7ecd7e4cd2c942d8`, run as a standalone Node process against OpenClaw runtime modules. This was not Vitest, CI, snapshots, or mocked test-runner output. The proof registered a real context-engine implementation, resolved it through the real context-engine registry using `plugins.slots.contextEngine`, then drove the real harness lifecycle functions.
- **Exact steps or command run after fix**: From the final-head checkout, run `node --import tsx --input-type=module` with a standalone proof script that registers a context engine, resolves it through `resolveContextEngine({ plugins: { slots: { contextEngine: engineId } } })`, and invokes `bootstrapHarnessContextEngine`, `assembleHarnessContextEngine`, `finalizeHarnessContextEngineTurn`, and `compactContextEngineWithSafetyTimeout`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Copied terminal output from the final-head standalone run, with session identifiers and message text redacted:

```json
{
  "proof": "real configured context-engine lifecycle runtimeSettings proof",
  "head": "a767005b615a2e8eb6400d5c7ecd7e4cd2c942d8",
  "configuredEngineId": "real-runtime-settings-proof-1780283608145",
  "commandKind": "standalone node execution against OpenClaw runtime modules, not Vitest or CI",
  "hooksObserved": [
    "bootstrap",
    "maintain",
    "assemble",
    "afterTurn",
    "maintain",
    "compact"
  ],

- **Observed result after fix**: The configured context engine was selected by the registry from `plugins.slots.contextEngine`. Every required lifecycle path produced `runtimeSettings.schemaVersion: 1`, fallback mode, requested/resolved model split, provider, `family: null`, `fallbackActive: true`, fallback reason, and embedded host identity. Prompt-budgeted hooks saw `tokenBudget: 2048`; bootstrap/startup maintenance correctly had no prompt budget yet.
- **What was not tested**: This final-head proof did not restart the production Gateway. Earlier live Gateway validation remains supplemental because it predates final head. This proof is the current-head real configured-engine execution ClawSweeper asked for.

## Review Findings Addressed

- [P1] Strict-schema legacy engines could quarantine on the new `runtimeSettings` key. Fixed by adding `runtimeSettings` to the registry legacy strip/retry set for bootstrap, maintain, afterTurn, assemble, and compact. Covered by focused compatibility tests. Commit: `14e1385788`.
- [P1] Current-head configured-plugin proof was missing. Added `passes runtime settings through a configured context engine across lifecycle hooks`, which registers an engine, resolves it through `plugins.slots.contextEngine`, and verifies runtimeSettings on bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintenance, and compact. Commit: `9de0196a4b`.
- [P2] Overflow compaction built runtimeSettings for `compact()` but dropped them on immediate post-compaction maintenance. Fixed by reusing the same runtimeSettings object for both calls and covering the handoff in `run.overflow-compaction.test.ts`. Commit: `a767005b61`.
- [P2] `model.family` could mirror a concrete model id. Fixed by defaulting family to `null` unless a real family is supplied. Covered by regression tests. Commits: `2f1c92fc28`, `08ae320eca`.
- [P2] SDK contract was exported but not documented. Fixed by documenting `runtimeSettings` in context-engine docs and SDK overview. Commit: `14e1385788`.
- [P2] `compact()` documented `runtimeSettings`, but real compact callers did not pass it. Fixed embedded timeout/overflow compaction, queued/manual embedded compaction, CLI compaction, and follow-up maintenance to pass typed runtime settings. Commits: `b15732c179`, `a767005b61`.
- [P2] Production embedded paths did not populate requested/resolved/fallback/degraded fields. Fixed embedded attempts to capture requested model and pass fallback metadata through bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, and maintenance. Commit: `b15732c179`.
- CI failure in `checks-fast-contracts-plugins-b`: fixed `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts` to include `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnavailableError` and `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnsupportedError`. Commit: `b15732c179`.
- Copilot review: `fallbackReason` and `fallbackActive` could be inconsistent. Fixed by deriving `fallbackActive` from any non-empty fallback reason. Covered by regression test. Commit: `14e1385788`.
- Copilot review: runtime-settings Error subclasses lacked names. Fixed. Commit: `2f1c92fc28`.
- Copilot review: missing builder test branches for degraded mode and invalid capabilities. Fixed. Commit: `14e1385788`.

## Current-Head Proof

Environment: OpenClaw source tree for this PR branch, run locally against current head `a767005b615a2e8eb6400d5c7ecd7e4cd2c942d8`. Proof output is sanitized: no home paths, tokens, private endpoints, or raw transcripts are included here.

Commands run at current head:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts src/context-engine/runtime-settings.test.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts
pnpm check:test-types
pnpm build
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1
```

Observed result:

```text
plugin-sdk-index.test.ts: 4 passed
context-engine.test.ts: 42 passed
runtime-settings.test.ts: 6 passed
context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts: 12 passed
run.overflow-compaction.test.ts: included in focused agent shard; focused agent shard 52 passed
pnpm check:test-types: exit 0
pnpm build: exit 0, including check-plugin-sdk-exports OK
git diff --check: clean
contracts-plugin config: 64 files, 894 tests passed
```

Configured context-engine proof added in `context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts`:

- registers a test context engine with bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintain, and compact hooks
- resolves that engine through `plugins.slots.contextEngine`
- drives the resolved engine through bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn/finalization maintenance, and compact timeout wrapper
- asserts every captured hook receives schema version 1 runtimeSettings
- asserts fallback mode, requested/resolved model split, provider, `family: null`, `fallbackActive: true`, and `fallbackReason`

Overflow compaction proof added in `run.overflow-compaction.test.ts`:

- drives overflow recovery through the embedded runner harness
- asserts the same runtimeSettings object reaches engine-owned overflow `compact()` and follow-up `runContextEngineMaintenance()`
- asserts post-compaction maintenance sees degraded mode and `degradedReason: "context_overflow"`

Compatibility proof added in `context-engine.test.ts`:

- strict legacy `assemble()` retries without `sessionKey`, `prompt`, and `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `bootstrap()` retries without `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `maintain()` retries without `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `afterTurn()` retries without `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `compact()` retries without `runtimeSettings`

Runtime-settings proof added in `runtime-settings.test.ts` and `context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts`:

- `model.family` defaults to `null` and never mirrors a concrete model id
- explicit `modelFamily` is preserved when supplied
- `fallbackReason` forces `fallbackActive: true` and `runtime.mode: "fallback"`
- `degradedReason` yields `runtime.mode: "degraded"`
- invalid host capabilities fail closed with `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnsupportedError`

## Compatibility and Risk

`runtimeSettings` remains optional on every lifecycle hook param. Engines that ignore it continue unchanged. Engines with strict unknown-field validation now get the same compatibility retry behavior OpenClaw already uses for legacy `sessionKey` and `prompt` additions.

Remaining review risk: current-head live Gateway proof is still a maintainer policy question. The branch now has current-head source-level configured-engine proof and current-head focused/full local validation, and it no longer contains the unrelated provider-default change.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts src/context-engine/runtime-settings.test.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts` -> pass, focused shards including 52 agent tests.
- `pnpm check:test-types` -> pass.
- `pnpm build` -> pass.
- `git diff --check` -> clean.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` -> pass, 894 tests total.

## Operator Understanding

ragesaq asked for proof from actual use before merge, called out that labels still required proof/triage proof, asked for PR follow-up and cleanup, then asked to clean up the unrelated Gemini commit. The PR has been rewritten onto the current release base so the public diff is now scoped to runtime settings only.

target.title: feat(context-engine): pass runtime settings into lifecycle
target.body:
## Summary

AI-assisted change: yes. Forge/Codex produced the implementation and PR shepherding with OpenAI `gpt-5.5`; ragesaq reviewed the direction and required production-grade proof before merge.

Pass a typed `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` contract through the context-engine lifecycle for embedded, CLI, and Codex app-server runs. Context engines now receive a versioned snapshot of runtime mode, model/provider state, host capabilities, token limits, and diagnostics instead of only the assemble-time `model` string.

## Changes

- Add `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` and `ContextEngineRuntimeMode` in `src/context-engine/types.ts`.
- Add `buildContextEngineRuntimeSettings()` in `src/context-engine/runtime-settings.ts`.
- Thread `runtimeSettings` through bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, compact, and maintenance paths.
- Export the runtime-settings contract and errors through the public plugin SDK barrel.
- Preserve strict legacy context engines: if an engine rejects `runtimeSettings` as an unknown key, OpenClaw retries the lifecycle call without it instead of quarantining the engine.
- Document the public context-engine runtime-settings contract in `docs/concepts/context-engine.md` and `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`.
- Update the plugin SDK export contract test for the new public runtime-settings error exports.
- Add configured-slot proof that a resolved context engine receives runtimeSettings across bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintenance, and compact.
- Preserve the same overflow compaction runtimeSettings into the immediately-following post-compaction maintenance call.

## Current Head

Current PR head: `a767005b615a2e8eb6400d5c7ecd7e4cd2c942d8`.

Scope cleanup: the unrelated Copilot Gemini model-id commit `747f09e19610` has been removed from this PR by rewriting the branch over the current `release/2026.5.31` base. The current PR stack is 8 context-engine/runtime-settings commits, 20 files changed, and no `extensions/github-copilot` files in the diff.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Context engines configured through `plugins.slots.contextEngine` receive the typed `runtimeSettings` payload after the fix across bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintenance, and compact lifecycle hooks.
- **Real environment tested**: Final-head OpenClaw checkout at `a767005b615a2e8eb6400d5c7ecd7e4cd2c942d8`, run as a standalone Node process against OpenClaw runtime modules. This was not Vitest, CI, snapshots, or mocked test-runner output. The proof registered a real context-engine implementation, resolved it through the real context-engine registry using `plugins.slots.contextEngine`, then drove the real harness lifecycle functions.
- **Exact steps or command run after fix**: From the final-head checkout, run `node --import tsx --input-type=module` with a standalone proof script that registers a context engine, resolves it through `resolveContextEngine({ plugins: { slots: { contextEngine: engineId } } })`, and invokes `bootstrapHarnessContextEngine`, `assembleHarnessContextEngine`, `finalizeHarnessContextEngineTurn`, and `compactContextEngineWithSafetyTimeout`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Copied terminal output from the final-head standalone run, with session identifiers and message text redacted:

```json

  "proof": "real configured context-engine lifecycle runtimeSettings proof",
  "head": "a767005b615a2e8eb6400d5c7ecd7e4cd2c942d8",

Prompt-budgeted hooks saw `tokenBudget: 2048`; bootstrap/startup maintenance correctly had no prompt budget yet.
- **What was not tested**: This final-head proof did not restart the production Gateway. Earlier live Gateway validation remains supplemental because it predates final head. This proof is the current-head real configured-engine execution ClawSweeper asked for.

## Review Findings Addressed

- [P1] Strict-schema legacy engines could quarantine on the new `runtimeSettings` key. Fixed by adding `runtimeSettings` to the registry legacy strip/retry set for bootstrap, maintain, afterTurn, assemble, and compact. Covered by focused compatibility tests. Commit: `14e1385788`.

- [P2] Overflow compaction built runtimeSettings for `compact()` but dropped them on immediate post-compaction maintenance. Fixed by reusing the same runtimeSettings object for both calls and covering the handoff in `run.overflow-compaction.test.ts`. Commit: `a767005b61`.

- CI failure in `checks-fast-contracts-plugins-b`: fixed `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts` to include `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnavailableError` and `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnsupportedError`. Commit: `b15732c179`.
- Copilot review: `fallbackReason` and `fallbackActive` could be inconsistent. Fixed by deriving `fallbackActive` from any non-empty fallback reason. Covered by regression test. Commit: `14e1385788`.
- Copilot review: runtime-settings Error subclasses lacked names. Fixed. Commit: `2f1c92fc28`.
- Copilot review: missing builder test branches for degraded mode and invalid capabilities. Fixed. Commit: `14e1385788`.

## Current-Head Proof

Remaining review risk: current-head live Gateway proof is still a maintainer policy question. The branch now has current-head source-level configured-engine proof and current-head focused/full local validation, and it no longer contains the unrelated provider-default change.

target.title: fix(telegram): slow polling restart storms
target.body:
## Summary
- Increase Telegram polling restart backoff from the tight 2s/30s policy to a bounded 30s/10m exponential policy.
- Deduplicate runner/bot stop calls per polling restart cycle so watchdog restarts do not stack repeated stop requests.
- Add stop-timeout burst cooldown and reset restart state after healthy normal and isolated-ingress polling cycles, including isolated forced-restart stop timeouts.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed:** Consecutive Telegram polling stop timeouts no longer restart on the same short 30s ceiling. The second stop-timeout burst applies a separate cooldown, while a healthy normal or isolated polling cycle resets restart attempts back to the first-delay path.
- **Real environment tested:** Isolated PR-head worktree `/private/tmp/openclaw-pr88832-proof` at `0579b14240079e4c791646a5b09e4bf39f8bff15`, running the actual `TelegramPollingSession` isolated-ingress restart loop, actual wall-clock timers, actual restart/backoff helper, and a real grammY `Bot` instance pointed at a refused local Telegram API endpoint (`127.0.0.1:9`) with a redacted dummy token. The ingress worker was production-like but local: it emitted a real `poll-start`, held the in-flight getUpdates open, and resolved `stop()` only after the 15s stop grace so the session exercised the forced stop-timeout path without using production credentials.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran the stop-timeout/network-flap proof below through `node --import tsx proof-telegram-stop-timeout.mjs`, ran the helper proof below through `node --import tsx`, then ran focused Vitest cases against `extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts` that exercise normal restart backoff, isolated `poll-success` reset, and two consecutive isolated forced stop-timeout restarts reaching the cooldown policy.
- **Evidence after fix:** Helper output from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
firstStopTimeoutDelayMs=33000
firstStopTimeoutSuffix=""
secondStopTimeoutDelayMs=132000
secondStopTimeoutSuffix=" Stop timeout burst=2; applying cooldown."
afterHealthyDelayMs=33000
afterHealthySuffix=""
```

- **Production-like stop-timeout/network-flap proof from PR head:**

```text
$ node --import tsx proof-telegram-stop-timeout.mjs

+30.1s [telegram] Polling stall detected (active getUpdates stuck for 60.98s); forcing restart. [diag inFlight=1 outcome=started startedAt=<redacted> finishedAt=n/a durationMs=n/a offset=n/a]
+45.1s [telegram] Isolated polling ingress stop timed out after 15s; forcing restart cycle.
+45.1s Telegram isolated polling ingress restart requested; restarting in 33.53s.
+108.7s [telegram] Polling stall detected (active getUpdates stuck for 60.98s); forcing restart. [diag inFlight=1 outcome=started startedAt=<redacted> finishedAt=n/a durationMs=n/a offset=n/a]
+123.7s [telegram] Isolated polling ingress stop timed out after 15s; forcing restart cycle.
+123.7s Telegram isolated polling ingress restart requested; restarting in 124.2s. Stop timeout burst=2; applying cooldown.
PROOF_SUMMARY_START
head=0579b14240079e4c791646a5b09e4bf39f8bff15
workerCycles=2
containsStopTimeoutCooldown=true
elapsedMs=124702
PROOF_SUMMARY_END
```

- **Production-like isolated polling focused test proof:**

```text
$ node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts -t "drains Telegram delivery queue after isolated ingress reports poll success|resets restart backoff after isolated ingress reports poll success|applies stop-timeout cooldown to isolated ingress forced restarts|uses backoff helpers|resets restart backoff|cools down repeated|forces a restart when polling stalls without getUpdates activity|forces a restart when the runner task is pending" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000

 RUN  v4.1.7 /private/tmp/openclaw-pr88832-proof

··----··-·-------------------------···---------------

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  8 passed | 45 skipped (53)
   Start at  18:42:52
   Duration  6.63s (transform 5.09s, setup 193ms, import 166ms, tests 6.19s, environment 0ms)
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The first stop timeout uses the normal 30s-class restart policy, the second consecutive stop timeout is raised by the stop-timeout cooldown path, the normal healthy cycle resets to the first-attempt delay, isolated-ingress `poll-success` resets the real session backoff state, and isolated forced stop-timeout restarts now wait through the same cooldown/backoff path instead of immediately continuing.
- **What was not tested:** No production Telegram bot token was used and no message was sent. The production-like proof intentionally used a local refused Telegram API endpoint and a redacted dummy token to avoid credential exposure or disturbing the running gateway. The full local `polling-session.test.ts` file also hits an existing `upstream/main` hang in `keeps active spooled lanes blocked across isolated ingress restarts`; this was reproduced in a detached `upstream/main` worktree, so verification used focused polling-session cases plus `monitor.test`.

## Verification
- `node --import tsx proof-telegram-stop-timeout.mjs`
- `node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts -t "drains Telegram delivery queue after isolated ingress reports poll success|resets restart backoff after isolated ingress reports poll success|applies stop-timeout cooldown to isolated ingress forced restarts|uses backoff helpers|resets restart backoff|cools down repeated|forces a restart when polling stalls without getUpdates activity|forces a restart when the runner task is pending" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000`
- `node --no-maglev ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-telegram.config.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts -t "applies stop-timeout cooldown to isolated ingress forced restarts|restarts isolated ingress when worker liveness stalls|resets restart backoff after isolated ingress reports poll success|cools down repeated" --reporter=dot --testTimeout=30000 --hookTimeout=30000`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/monitor.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm run lint:extensions:bundled`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.ts extensions/telegram/src/polling-session.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD`

Refs #88256

target.title: feat(context-engine): pass runtime settings into lifecycle
target.body:
## Summary

AI-assisted change: yes. Forge/Codex produced the implementation and PR shepherding with OpenAI `gpt-5.5`; ragesaq reviewed the direction and required production-grade proof before merge.

Pass a typed `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` contract through the context-engine lifecycle for embedded, CLI, and Codex app-server runs. Context engines now receive a versioned snapshot of runtime mode, model/provider state, host capabilities, token limits, and diagnostics instead of only the assemble-time `model` string.

## Changes

- Add `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` and `ContextEngineRuntimeMode` in `src/context-engine/types.ts`.
- Add `buildContextEngineRuntimeSettings()` in `src/context-engine/runtime-settings.ts`.
- Thread `runtimeSettings` through bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, compact, and maintenance paths.
- Export the runtime-settings contract and errors through the public plugin SDK barrel.
- Preserve strict legacy context engines: if an engine rejects `runtimeSettings` as an unknown key, OpenClaw retries the lifecycle call without it instead of quarantining the engine.
- Document the public context-engine runtime-settings contract in `docs/concepts/context-engine.md` and `docs/plugins/sdk-overview.md`.
- Update the plugin SDK export contract test for the new public runtime-settings error exports.
- Add configured-slot proof that a resolved context engine receives runtimeSettings across bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintenance, and compact.
- Preserve the same overflow compaction runtimeSettings into the immediately-following post-compaction maintenance call.

## Current Head

Current PR head: `a767005b615a2e8eb6400d5c7ecd7e4cd2c942d8`.

Scope cleanup: the unrelated Copilot Gemini model-id commit `747f09e19610` has been removed from this PR by rewriting the branch over the current `release/2026.5.31` base. The current PR stack is 8 context-engine/runtime-settings commits, 20 files changed, and no `extensions/github-copilot` files in the diff.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Context engines configured through `plugins.slots.contextEngine` receive the typed `runtimeSettings` payload after the fix across bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintenance, and compact lifecycle hooks.
- **Real environment tested**: Final-head OpenClaw checkout at `a767005b615a2e8eb6400d5c7ecd7e4cd2c942d8`, run as a standalone Node process against OpenClaw runtime modules. This was not Vitest, CI, snapshots, or mocked test-runner output. The proof registered a real context-engine implementation, resolved it through the real context-engine registry using `plugins.slots.contextEngine`, then drove the real harness lifecycle functions.
- **Exact steps or command run after fix**: From the final-head checkout, run `node --import tsx --input-type=module` with a standalone proof script that registers a context engine, resolves it through `resolveContextEngine({ plugins: { slots: { contextEngine: engineId } } })`, and invokes `bootstrapHarnessContextEngine`, `assembleHarnessContextEngine`, `finalizeHarnessContextEngineTurn`, and `compactContextEngineWithSafetyTimeout`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Copied terminal output from the final-head standalone run, with session identifiers and message text redacted:

```json
{
  "proof": "real configured context-engine lifecycle runtimeSettings proof",
  "head": "a767005b615a2e8eb6400d5c7ecd7e4cd2c942d8",
  "configuredEngineId": "real-runtime-settings-proof-1780283608145",
  "commandKind": "standalone node execution against OpenClaw runtime modules, not Vitest or CI",
  "hooksObserved": [
    "bootstrap",
    "maintain",
    "assemble",
    "afterTurn",
    "maintain",
    "compact"
  ],
  "missingHooks": [],
  "badEntries": [],
  "missingPromptBudgets": [],
  "runtimeSettingsByHook": [
    {
      "hook": "bootstrap",
      "schemaVersion": 1,
      "mode": "fallback",
      "provider": "openai",
      "requested": "openai/gpt-5.5",
      "resolved": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6",
      "family": null,
      "fallbackActive": true,
      "fallbackReason": "primary_provider_5xx",
      "hostId": "openclaw-embedded",

    },
    {

```

- **Observed result after fix**: The configured context engine was selected by the registry from `plugins.slots.contextEngine`. Every required lifecycle path produced `runtimeSettings.schemaVersion: 1`, fallback mode, requested/resolved model split, provider, `family: null`, `fallbackActive: true`, fallback reason, and embedded host identity. Prompt-budgeted hooks saw `tokenBudget: 2048`; bootstrap/startup maintenance correctly had no prompt budget yet.
- **What was not tested**: This final-head proof did not restart the production Gateway. Earlier live Gateway validation remains supplemental because it predates final head. This proof is the current-head real configured-engine execution ClawSweeper asked for.

## Review Findings Addressed

- [P1] Strict-schema legacy engines could quarantine on the new `runtimeSettings` key. Fixed by adding `runtimeSettings` to the registry legacy strip/retry set for bootstrap, maintain, afterTurn, assemble, and compact. Covered by focused compatibility tests. Commit: `14e1385788`.
- [P1] Current-head configured-plugin proof was missing. Added `passes runtime settings through a configured context engine across lifecycle hooks`, which registers an engine, resolves it through `plugins.slots.contextEngine`, and verifies runtimeSettings on bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintenance, and compact. Commit: `9de0196a4b`.
- [P2] Overflow compaction built runtimeSettings for `compact()` but dropped them on immediate post-compaction maintenance. Fixed by reusing the same runtimeSettings object for both calls and covering the handoff in `run.overflow-compaction.test.ts`. Commit: `a767005b61`.
- [P2] `model.family` could mirror a concrete model id. Fixed by defaulting family to `null` unless a real family is supplied. Covered by regression tests. Commits: `2f1c92fc28`, `08ae320eca`.
- [P2] SDK contract was exported but not documented. Fixed by documenting `runtimeSettings` in context-engine docs and SDK overview. Commit: `14e1385788`.
- [P2] `compact()` documented `runtimeSettings`, but real compact callers did not pass it. Fixed embedded timeout/overflow compaction, queued/manual embedded compaction, CLI compaction, and follow-up maintenance to pass typed runtime settings. Commits: `b15732c179`, `a767005b61`.
- [P2] Production embedded paths did not populate requested/resolved/fallback/degraded fields. Fixed embedded attempts to capture requested model and pass fallback metadata through bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, and maintenance. Commit: `b15732c179`.
- CI failure in `checks-fast-contracts-plugins-b`: fixed `src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts` to include `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnavailableError` and `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnsupportedError`. Commit: `b15732c179`.
- Copilot review: `fallbackReason` and `fallbackActive` could be inconsistent. Fixed by deriving `fallbackActive` from any non-empty fallback reason. Covered by regression test. Commit: `14e1385788`.
- Copilot review: runtime-settings Error subclasses lacked names. Fixed. Commit: `2f1c92fc28`.
- Copilot review: missing builder test branches for degraded mode and invalid capabilities. Fixed. Commit: `14e1385788`.

## Current-Head Proof

Environment: OpenClaw source tree for this PR branch, run locally against current head `a767005b615a2e8eb6400d5c7ecd7e4cd2c942d8`. Proof output is sanitized: no home paths, tokens, private endpoints, or raw transcripts are included here.

Commands run at current head:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts src/context-engine/runtime-settings.test.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts
pnpm check:test-types
pnpm build
git diff --check
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1
```

Observed result:

```text
plugin-sdk-index.test.ts: 4 passed
context-engine.test.ts: 42 passed
runtime-settings.test.ts: 6 passed
context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts: 12 passed
run.overflow-compaction.test.ts: included in focused agent shard; focused agent shard 52 passed
pnpm check:test-types: exit 0
pnpm build: exit 0, including check-plugin-sdk-exports OK
git diff --check: clean
contracts-plugin config: 64 files, 894 tests passed
```

Configured context-engine proof added in `context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts`:

- registers a test context engine with bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn, maintain, and compact hooks
- resolves that engine through `plugins.slots.contextEngine`
- drives the resolved engine through bootstrap, assemble, afterTurn/finalization maintenance, and compact timeout wrapper
- asserts every captured hook receives schema version 1 runtimeSettings
- asserts fallback mode, requested/resolved model split, provider, `family: null`, `fallbackActive: true`, and `fallbackReason`

Overflow compaction proof added in `run.overflow-compaction.test.ts`:

- drives overflow recovery through the embedded runner harness
- asserts the same runtimeSettings object reaches engine-owned overflow `compact()` and follow-up `runContextEngineMaintenance()`
- asserts post-compaction maintenance sees degraded mode and `degradedReason: "context_overflow"`

Compatibility proof added in `context-engine.test.ts`:

- strict legacy `assemble()` retries without `sessionKey`, `prompt`, and `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `bootstrap()` retries without `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `maintain()` retries without `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `afterTurn()` retries without `runtimeSettings`
- strict legacy `compact()` retries without `runtimeSettings`

Runtime-settings proof added in `runtime-settings.test.ts` and `context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts`:

- `model.family` defaults to `null` and never mirrors a concrete model id
- explicit `modelFamily` is preserved when supplied
- `fallbackReason` forces `fallbackActive: true` and `runtime.mode: "fallback"`
- `degradedReason` yields `runtime.mode: "degraded"`
- invalid host capabilities fail closed with `ContextEngineRuntimeSettingsUnsupportedError`

## Compatibility and Risk

`runtimeSettings` remains optional on every lifecycle hook param. Engines that ignore it continue unchanged. Engines with strict unknown-field validation now get the same compatibility retry behavior OpenClaw already uses for legacy `sessionKey` and `prompt` additions.

Remaining review risk: current-head live Gateway proof is still a maintainer policy question. The branch now has current-head source-level configured-engine proof and current-head focused/full local validation, and it no longer contains the unrelated provider-default change.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts src/context-engine/context-engine.test.ts src/context-engine/runtime-settings.test.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.test.ts` -> pass, focused shards including 52 agent tests.

target.title: fix(update): detect npm shrinkwrap package installs
target.body:
## Summary
- treat `npm-shrinkwrap.json` as npm install evidence for published npm package roots, even when package.json carries build-time `packageManager: pnpm`
- preserve pnpm package-root ownership when pnpm global/project layout proves it owns a package root that also carries `npm-shrinkwrap.json`
- preserve pnpm sibling virtual-store roots shaped like `.../5/.pnpm/.../node_modules/openclaw` by mapping them back to the owning `.../5/node_modules` layout
- preserve Bun global package-root ownership even when the published package metadata still says `pnpm`
- use `npm-shrinkwrap.json` as the npm dependency lock marker when present so update/status reports do not look for the wrong lockfile marker

Fixes #87732.

## Surface area
- No public config changes.
- No plugin API or plugin contract changes.
- No new CLI flags, env vars, migrations, or public update/config surface.
- No update command behavior rewrite; this only changes status/install detection and dependency marker reporting.

## Real npm-installed proof

ClawSweeper asked for proof from a real package-style npm install, not only synthetic temp roots. I built the PR head, packed the package, installed that tarball with npm into an isolated global prefix, then ran the installed `openclaw` binary from the installed package root.

Environment:
- WSL2 Ubuntu checkout: `/tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-refresh`
- Signed PR head tested: `090c9a955061ba844a1b55b1834634c63c2db344`
- Local validation base contained: `origin/main` `2d0c7550131d25f5c5f61a802c510d4d640f1260`
- Packed tarball: `/tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-npm-proof/pack/openclaw-2026.5.31.tgz`
- npm global prefix: `/tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-npm-proof/npm-prefix`
- Installed package root: `/tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-npm-proof/npm-prefix/lib/node_modules/openclaw`
- Installed shrinkwrap marker present: `/tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-npm-proof/npm-prefix/lib/node_modules/openclaw/npm-shrinkwrap.json` (`126321` bytes)

Exact proof commands:
```bash
CI=true pnpm run build
npm pack --pack-destination /tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-npm-proof/pack
npm install --global --prefix /tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-npm-proof/npm-prefix /tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-npm-proof/pack/openclaw-2026.5.31.tgz
cd /tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-npm-proof/npm-prefix/lib/node_modules/openclaw
OPENCLAW_DISABLE_AUTO_UPDATE=1 HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-npm-proof/home OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-npm-proof/openclaw-home /tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-npm-proof/npm-prefix/bin/openclaw update status --json > /tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-npm-proof/update-status-package-root.json
OPENCLAW_DISABLE_AUTO_UPDATE=1 HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-npm-proof/home OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-npm-proof/openclaw-home /tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-npm-proof/npm-prefix/bin/openclaw status --all --json > /tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-npm-proof/status-all-package-root.json
```

Observed proof output in both `update status --json` and `status --all --json`:
- `update.installKind = "package"`
- `update.packageManager = "npm"`
- `update.deps.manager = "npm"`
- `update.deps.status = "ok"`
- `update.deps.lockfilePath = "/tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-npm-proof/npm-prefix/lib/node_modules/openclaw/npm-shrinkwrap.json"`
- `update.deps.markerPath = "/tmp/openclaw-pr-87778-npm-proof/npm-prefix/lib/node_modules/openclaw/node_modules"`

Note: plain `openclaw status --json` uses the fast status payload and does not include the full update details. The real proof therefore uses `openclaw update status --json` and `openclaw status --all --json`, which are the status/update surfaces that expose the package manager and dependency marker fields.

## Regression coverage
- npm shrinkwrap package roots detect npm and use `npm-shrinkwrap.json` as the npm lockfile marker.
- pnpm package roots still detect pnpm when pnpm owns the root.
- pnpm virtual-store and sibling virtual-store package roots still detect pnpm from the owning `node_modules` layout.
- local pnpm package roots stay pnpm.
- Bun-owned package roots stay Bun even when the published package metadata says `pnpm` and the root includes shrinkwrap.
- update/status JSON reports the dependency marker selected by the detected package manager.

## Validation
- `CI=true pnpm run build` -> passed
- real npm global install proof commands above -> passed
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/detect-package-manager.test.ts src/infra/update-check.test.ts src/infra/update-global.test.ts src/commands/status.update.test.ts src/commands/status-json-payload.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 5 files, 74 tests passed
- `CI=true pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/infra/detect-package-manager.ts src/infra/detect-package-manager.test.ts` -> passed
- `CI=true pnpm exec oxlint --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json src/infra/detect-package-manager.ts src/infra/detect-package-manager.test.ts` -> 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` -> passed
- `git diff --check` -> passed
- `/home/giodl/.local/toolchains/node-v22.22.3-linux-x64/bin/codex review --base origin/main` -> no actionable correctness issues found
- `git log --show-signature --format="%H %G? %s" origin/main..HEAD` -> all 5 commits have good signatures

Signed head: `090c9a955061ba844a1b55b1834634c63c2db344`. GitHub currently reports the PR as mergeable against `main`.

target.title: test: consolidate plugin registration contracts
target.body:
## Summary

- Consolidate bundled plugin registration contract wrappers into one table-driven suite.
- Move extension-local registration expectations into the shared contract case table.
- Delete duplicate extension and per-plugin core wrapper files while preserving registration coverage.

## Verification

Passed for this cleanup:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-registration.contract.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/plugin-registration.contract.test.ts"`
- `git diff --check`

Additional lint signal:

- `node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs` passed before the current base-branch Microsoft Foundry lint drift.
- On the current base, the same bundled-extension lint command fails only in `extensions/microsoft-foundry/provider.ts`, outside this PR's diff.

## Broad Extension Attempt

- Testbox-through-Blacksmith `tbx_01kt0bsg5r8tzpkgmq9s43t3hz` ran `corepack pnpm test extensions` against this branch's synced content.
- The broad run did not pass, but failures were outside this PR's touched registration-contract files:
  - `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/events.test.ts`
  - `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.test.ts`
  - `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-embedding-timeout.test.ts`
  - `extensions/openrouter/music-generation-provider.test.ts`
  - `extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts`
  - final stuck/OOM state in `test/vitest/vitest.extension-codex.config.ts`
- The previous unrelated `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` failure passed in this broad run.

## Granularity Note

The one consolidated file still reports failures under per-plugin `describe` names from `describePluginRegistrationContract`, e.g. `<pluginId> plugin registration contract`, so the lost granularity is file-level only. The assertion names and plugin id remain visible in Vitest output.

## Maintainer Decision Needed

ClawSweeper's remaining P1s are maintainer acceptance items rather than patchable defects:

- **Test topology:** this PR intentionally removes per-plugin wrapper file paths. Accepting the PR means accepting centralized file-level discovery, with plugin identity preserved in `describe` names and assertions.
- **Broad proof gap:** the broad Testbox extension run did not pass, but the observed failures were outside touched registration-contract files. Merge should either accept that gap for this test-only cleanup or wait for a refreshed broad extension run.

Current CI also reports bundled extension lint failures in `extensions/microsoft-foundry/provider.ts`; those errors are unrelated to this PR's diff and come from the current base branch, not the registration-contract cleanup.

target.title: feat: channel echo / session pinning
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds echo delivery infrastructure that mirrors session messages to additional channels
- `SessionEchoTarget` type on `SessionEntry` for persistent echo target configuration
- `message:sent` internal hook triggers echo delivery (fire-and-forget, no circular echo)
- CLI: `openclaw sessions echo add/remove/list` subcommands
- Gateway method: `sessions.echo` for programmatic pin/unpin (VituReClaw HUD)

## Motivation

Sessions used across multiple transports (Telegram + VituReClaw glasses + web UI) result in fragmented chat logs. Echo targets let every turn appear in all pinned channels regardless of originating transport.

## Files changed

| File | Purpose |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/sessions/types.ts` | `SessionEchoTarget` type + `echoTargets` field |
| `src/infra/outbound/echo.ts` | Core echo resolution + delivery |
| `src/infra/outbound/echo-hook.ts` | Internal hook registration |
| `src/commands/sessions-echo.ts` | CLI subcommands |
| `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts` | `sessions.echo` gateway method |
| `src/gateway/server-methods.ts` | Method registration |
| `src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts` | Hook auto-registration at boot |
| `src/cli/program/register.status-health-sessions.ts` | CLI command wiring |

## Test plan

- [ ] Unit test for `resolveEchoTargets` (deduplication, role filtering)
- [ ] Integration test: add echo target via gateway method, send message, verify echo arrives
- [ ] Verify circular echo prevention (echo delivery does not re-trigger hooks)
- [ ] CLI smoke test: add/list/remove echo targets

Supersedes #88386 (closed: branch had unrelated deployment commits).

target.title: fix(browser): remove unused requireRef import and void workaround (#83878)
target.body:
## Summary

Removes the unused `requireRef` import and its `void requireRef` silencing workaround from `register.navigation.ts`. The import serves no purpose — the file does not call `requireRef`, and documenting the public surface of `shared.ts` belongs in `shared.ts` itself or a barrel export, not as a phantom import.

Closes #83878

## Change Type

- [x] Code cleanup / Dead code removal

## Scope

- [x] Browser extension / CLI

## Linked Issue

- Closes #83878

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `register.navigation.ts` imports `requireRef` from `./shared.js` but never calls it. The `void requireRef` expression at line 84 silences the unused-import lint without creating a real dependency. This is dead code that obscures the actual import graph.

**Real environment tested**: macOS Darwin 25.4.0 arm64, Node v22.22.0, openclaw branch `fix/remove-void-require-ref` rebased on upstream main commit `8e110a21`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

1. Removed `requireRef` from the import and deleted the `void requireRef` + comment block (4 lines removed, 1 line changed).
2. Verified TypeScript compilation: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.json` — exit 0.
3. Verified formatting: `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.navigation.ts` — clean.
4. Verified linting: `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.navigation.ts` — 0 warnings, 0 errors.
5. Drift proof: the removed import (`requireRef`) and `void` expression do not exist in the patched version. Reverting the patch restores the dead import.

**Evidence after fix**:

```
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.json
# exit 0

$ pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.navigation.ts
Finished in 22ms on 1 files using 10 threads.

$ pnpm exec oxlint extensions/browser/src/cli/browser-cli-actions-input/register.navigation.ts
Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.
```

Diff (1 file, +1 / -4):
```diff
-import { requireRef, resolveBrowserActionContext } from "./shared.js";
+import { resolveBrowserActionContext } from "./shared.js";
...
-  // Keep `requireRef` reachable; shared utilities are intended for other modules too.
-  void requireRef;
```

**Observed result**: After the patch, `register.navigation.ts` no longer imports or references `requireRef`. TypeScript, format, and lint all pass cleanly. The file is functionally identical — `resolveBrowserActionContext` is still imported and used as before.

target.title: test: consolidate media capability assertions
target.body:
## Summary
- Consolidate repeated media provider explicit capability-shape checks into one extension-owned contract suite.
- Remove the redundant per-provider one-line capability tests while keeping provider-specific capability and behavior assertions in place.
- Leave the existing core media provider manifest contract test manifest-only to avoid core deep-importing plugin runtime modules.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media-generation/provider-capabilities.contract.test.ts extensions/media-generation-provider-capabilities.contract.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/alibaba/video-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/byteplus/video-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/comfy/music-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/comfy/video-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/deepinfra/video-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/fal/music-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/fal/video-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/google/music-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/google/video-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/minimax/music-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/minimax/video-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/openai/video-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/openrouter/music-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/openrouter/video-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/pixverse/video-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/qwen/video-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/runway/video-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/together/video-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/vydra/video-generation-provider.test.ts extensions/xai/video-generation-provider.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Consolidates redundant provider capability-shape tests without changing provider runtime behavior.
Real environment tested: Local Codex OpenClaw worktree on macOS.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: The verification commands listed above.
Evidence after fix: Contract suite passed 22 tests; touched provider batch passed 170 tests across 4 Vitest shards; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
Observed result after fix: The same generic media capability helper assertions now run from one extension-owned contract suite, and provider-specific tests still cover their local behavior and capability details.
What was not tested: Full repository check suite and remote/Testbox gates were not run for this test-only cleanup.

target.title: Add Vertex API key model config regression coverage
target.body:
## Summary
- Add regression coverage for the Google Vertex Express API-key/profile path from #88816.
- Verify models.json planning preserves `google-vertex/gemini-2.5-pro` provider rows when `agents.defaults.models` selects Vertex and the credential comes from an auth profile rather than `models.providers`.
- Builds on current `main`'s Vertex static catalog rows so the reported `model_not_found` hint does not return for the profile-backed config shape.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed:** Google Vertex model config generation keeps `google-vertex` catalog rows for profile-backed API key auth, so `agents.defaults.models["google-vertex/gemini-2.5-pro"]` has a matching `models.providers["google-vertex"].models[]` row.
- **Real environment tested:** Local checkout `/Users/andy/openclaw` on branch `codex/google-vertex-express-catalog`, using the actual Google static catalog builder and models.json planner against a temp agent auth-profile setup.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx` one-shot script that wrote `auth-profiles.json` with `google-vertex:default`, loaded `buildGoogleVertexStaticCatalogProvider()`, ran `planOpenClawModelsJsonWithDeps(...)`, and inspected the generated provider row.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the local OpenClaw setup:

```text
agentDir=/var/folders/sl/5dkd3zq12dv65j6jx57zq1hc0000gn/T/openclaw-vertex-proof-i6CI6m
planAction=write
providerKeys=google-vertex
googleVertexApi=google-vertex
googleVertexApiKey=GOOGLE_CLOUD_API_KEY
googleVertexHasGemini25Pro=true
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The generated provider config contains the `google-vertex` provider row, preserves the API-key marker from the profile, and includes the `gemini-2.5-pro` model row needed by the reported `agents.defaults.models` selection.
- **What was not tested:** No live Vertex Express network request was run because no Vertex Express credentials were available in this checkout.

## Additional verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/google/provider-models.test.ts src/agents/models-config.skips-writing-models-json-no-env-token.test.ts src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `git log --format='%h %an <%ae> %s' upstream/main..HEAD`

Refs #88816

target.title: fix: split image setup and request timeout semantics
target.body:
## Summary
- split image-description timeout handling so setup and provider request both use the configured `tools.media.image.timeoutSeconds` budget instead of shrinking the request budget by elapsed setup time
- preserve the full configured timeout for the provider request instead of silently subtracting setup latency down to a tiny remaining budget
- add focused tests covering request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and slow-setup request behavior
- keep public docs user-facing: the image timeout still applies to the request itself and is not reduced by earlier preparation work
- keep the heavier image-tool latency and runtime-loading reductions for follow-up PRs after this timeout-semantics fix

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider docker --docker-image node:22-bookworm --timing-json --shell -- "bash -lc 'corepack enable >/dev/null 2>&1 && env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 corepack pnpm test src/media-understanding/image.test.ts'"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: image timeout errors no longer collapse setup latency into a tiny remaining provider timeout; setup and request failures now report distinct phases.
Real environment tested: Crabbox `docker` provider with `node:22-bookworm`, synced from this worktree.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: run the targeted media-understanding Vitest file via Crabbox Docker using the command above.
Evidence after fix: `src/media-understanding/image.test.ts` passes with 25 tests; assertions cover request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and a slow setup that still passes the full configured timeout through to the provider request.
Observed result after fix: the test suite passed, provider-request calls still received `timeoutMs: 1000` after a mocked `400ms` setup delay, and timeout messages now distinguish `setup` vs `request`.
What was not tested: live provider behavior and the follow-up PRs that will reduce the image runtime-loading latency itself.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`
